=== Judges 11:31 — THE VOW APODOSIS (CENTRAL) === וְהָיָה הַיּוֹצֵא אֲשֶׁר יֵצֵא מִדַּלְתֵי בֵיתִי לִקְרָאתִי בְּשׁוּבִי בְשָׁלוֹם מִבְּנֵי עַמּוֹן וְהָיָה לַיהוָה וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ עוֹלָה

Word Lemma Parsing Gloss וְהָיָה היה Verb.Qal.Perf.3ms weqatal it shall be הַיּוֹצֵא יצא Verb.Qal.Ptcp.ms.Abs the one going out אֲשֶׁר יֵצֵא יצא relative + Impf.3ms who will go out מִדַּלְתֵי בֵיתִי from doors of my house לִקְרָאתִי קרא Qal.InfCon+1s to meet me בְּשׁוּבִי שׁוב Qal.InfCon+1s in my returning בְשָׁלוֹם מִבְּנֵי עַמּוֹן in peace from Ammon וְהָיָה היה Qal.Perf.3ms weqatal and shall be לַיהוָה יהוה to/for YHWH וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ עלה Verb.Hiphil.Perf.1s.+3ms and I will offer him/it up עוֹלָה עלה Noun.fs.Abs (as) burnt-offering

STRUCTURE: weqatal APODOSIS chain × 3 — וְהָיָה … וְהָיָה לַיהוָה … וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ עֹלָה GRAMMAR NOTE: Default force of third weqatal וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ after two conjunctive weqatals is ALSO conjunctive ("and I will offer it up"). Disjunctive "or" reading is a grammatically admissible but MARKED reading — waw polyfunctionality documented in W-O'C §39.2 and exemplified at Exo 21:15, 17 (noun-level "father OR mother"). Hebrew has a dedicated unambiguous disjunctive particle אוֹ ('o) but Jdg 11:31 does NOT use it. Neither LXX A-text (καί) nor B-text (asyndeton) read disjunction.

=== Judges 11:34 — Daughter Emerges; YECHIDAH Akedah Echo === וַיָּבֹא יִפְתָּח הַמִּצְפָּה אֶל־בֵּיתוֹ וְהִנֵּה בִתּוֹ יֹצֵאת לִקְרָאתוֹ בְתֻפִּים וּבִמְחֹלוֹת וְרַק הִיא יְחִידָה אֵין־לוֹ מִמֶּנּוּ בֵּן אוֹ־בַת

KEY: יֹצֵאת (Qal.Ptcp.fs.Abs "going out" — feminine counterpart of Jdg 11:31 הַיּוֹצֵא) KEY: יְחִידָה (Adj.fs.Abs "only one") — loaded lexical echo of Gen 22:2 יְחִידְךָ KEY: אוֹ־בַת — "or daughter" — uses explicit disjunctive אוֹ (contrast with 11:31 where waw is used, not אוֹ — so if Jdg 11:31 had meant "or" the author had the particle available and chose not to use it)

=== Judges 11:35 — Jephthah Tears Clothes === וַיְהִי כִרְאוֹתוֹ אוֹתָהּ וַיִּקְרַע אֶת־בְּגָדָיו וַיֹּאמֶר אֲהָהּ בִּתִּי הַכְרֵעַ הִכְרַעְתִּנִי וְאַתְּ הָיִיתְ בְּעֹכְרָי וְאָנֹכִי פָּצִיתִי־פִי אֶל־יְהוָה וְלֹא אוּכַל לָשׁוּב

KEY: הַכְרֵעַ הִכְרַעְתִּנִי — Hiphil inf.abs.+Hiphil perf.2fs+1s "thou hast utterly bowed me down" — tautological inf.abs. intensification in lament register KEY: פָּצִיתִי־פִי אֶל־יְהוָה — Qal.Perf.1s פצה "I have opened my mouth unto YHWH" KEY: וְלֹא אוּכַל לָשׁוּב — "I cannot go back/turn" — Jephthah's own self- assessment of the vow's irrevocability (BUT note: this is his subjective understanding, not a divine declaration; Lev 5:4-5 rash-oath guilt-offering and Lev 27:1-8 redemption schedule both provide Torah off-ramps he did not invoke)

=== Judges 11:36 — Daughter's Consent === וַתֹּאמֶר אֵלָיו אָבִי פָּצִיתָה אֶת־פִּיךָ אֶל־יְהוָה עֲשֵׂה לִי כַּאֲשֶׁר יָצָא מִפִּיךָ אַחֲרֵי אֲשֶׁר עָשָׂה לְךָ יְהוָה נְקָמוֹת מֵאֹיְבֶיךָ מִבְּנֵי עַמּוֹן

KEY: עֲשֵׂה (Qal.Impv.2ms) + לִי — do to me — same verb עשׂה as 11:39 climax KEY: כַּאֲשֶׁר יָצָא מִפִּיךָ — "according as it went out from thy mouth" Parallel to Num 30:2 "all that proceedeth out of his mouth" (legal frame) KEY: עָשָׂה לְךָ יְהוָה נְקָמוֹת — "YHWH has done vengeance for thee" — covenant vindication frame (not "YHWH demands") — the emphasis is on payment-of- vow, not on human-sacrifice requirement

=== Judges 11:37 — Daughter's Request to Mourn bethulim === וַתֹּאמֶר אֶל־אָבִיהָ יֵעָשֶׂה לִּי הַדָּבָר הַזֶּה הַרְפֵּה מִמֶּנִּי שְׁנַיִם חֳדָשִׁים וְאֵלְכָה וְיָרַדְתִּי עַל־הֶהָרִים וְאֶבְכֶּה עַל־בְּתוּלַי אָנֹכִי וְרֵעוֹתָי

KEY: יֵעָשֶׂה (Verb.Niphal.Impf.3ms) — "let it be done" (polite passive) KEY: עַל־בְּתוּלַי (Noun.fp.Abs.+1s בתולים) — "for MY VIRGINITY" Abstract plural בְּתוּלִים = "state of virginity" (not "virgin" = bethulah)

=== Judges 11:38 — Daughter Mourns bethulim on Mountains === וַיֹּאמֶר לֵכִי וַיִּשְׁלַח אוֹתָהּ שְׁנֵי חֳדָשִׁים וַתֵּלֶךְ הִיא וְרֵעוֹתֶיהָ וַתֵּבְךְּ עַל־בְּתוּלֶיהָ עַל־הֶהָרִים

KEY: וַתֵּבְךְּ עַל־בְּתוּלֶיהָ (Qal.Wayq.3fs + bethulim+3fs) — "she wept over her virginity-state" (narrator repeats noun from v.37 verbatim)

=== Judges 11:39 — FULFILLMENT + WX-QATAL (CENTRAL) === וַיְהִי מִקֵּץ שְׁנַיִם חֳדָשִׁים וַתָּשָׁב אֶל־אָבִיהָ וַיַּעַשׂ לָהּ אֶת־נִדְרוֹ אֲשֶׁר נָדָר וְהִיא לֹא־יָדְעָה אִישׁ וַתְּהִי־חֹק בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל

Word Lemma Parsing Gloss וַיְהִי היה Qal.Wayq.3ms and it came to pass מִקֵּץ שְׁנַיִם חֳדָשִׁים at end of two months וַתָּשָׁב שׁוב Qal.Wayq.3fs and she returned אֶל־אָבִיהָ to her father וַיַּעַשׂ עשׂה Qal.Wayq.3ms AND HE DID [neutral verb] לָהּ ל Prep+3fs to her אֶת־נִדְרוֹ נדר Noun.ms.Abs.+3ms his vow אֲשֶׁר נָדָר נדר Qal.Perf.3ms which he had vowed [cognate-accusative] וְהִיא היא PersPron.3fs and she [fronted — WX-QATAL] לֹא יָדְעָה ידע Qal.Perf.3fs had not known [stative perfect] אִישׁ אישׁ Noun.ms.Abs a man וַתְּהִי היה Qal.Wayq.3fs and it became חֹק חק Noun.ms.Abs a statute בְּיִשְׂרָאֵל in Israel

CRITICAL OBSERVATIONS ON JDG 11:39: 1. וַיַּעַשׂ לָהּ אֶת־נִדְרוֹ — "he did to her his vow." The narrator uses colorless עשׂה ("do/make") + dative construction, avoiding: - H2076 זבח (slaughter/sacrifice) — absent - H7819 שׁחט (slaughter technically) — absent - H8313 שׂרף (burn) — absent - H5927 Hiphil עלה ("offer up") + עלה ("burnt offering") — absent, though this is exactly the verb-cluster Jephthah himself used in 11:31 AND that 2 Kgs 3:27 uses of Mesha's identical act ("wayya'alehu olah"). - הֶעֱבִיר בָּאֵשׁ (pass through fire) — absent The lexical restraint is conspicuous against every canonical control for reported human immolation (see raw-data/verse-list.txt). The narrator names the act only by reference back to the vow-utterance, not by sacrificial verb.

  1. וְהִיא לֹא־יָדְעָה אִישׁ — WX-QATAL (waw + fronted subject-pronoun + qatal). This is NOT a wayyiqtol sequential clause. It BREAKS the wayyiqtol chain between וַיַּעַשׂ and וַתְּהִי. WX-QATAL signals circumstantial / backgrounded / stative-resultant information (W-O'C §39.2.3; GKC §156). Grammatical verdict from library entry (syntax-wx-qatal-circumstantial.md): "The clause is NOT a statement that her virginity continued after the vow's execution through a subsequent lifetime of celibacy (which would be the sequential reading); it is a background state-description framing the fulfillment of the vow." Whatever else it means, it is the narrator's interpretive gloss on "doing the vow" expressed in SEXUAL/MARITAL terms, not mortality terms.

  2. Feminine-subject + ידע + אישׁ euphemism: Gen 24:16 Rebekah (alive); Num 31:17-18, 35 Midianite women (alive or killed — explicitly spelled out); Jdg 21:12 Jabesh-Gilead virgins (alive); 1 Kgs 1:4 Abishag (alive) — EVERY OT occurrence of the feminine "lo yada ish" formula describes LIVING women's sexual status. No OT occurrence describes a dead woman.

=== Judges 11:40 — Annual Rite === מִיָּמִים יָמִימָה תֵּלַכְנָה בְּנוֹת יִשְׂרָאֵל לְתַנּוֹת לְבַת־יִפְתָּח הַגִּלְעָדִי אַרְבַּעַת יָמִים בַּשָּׁנָה

KEY: לְתַנּוֹת (Verb.Piel.InfCon תנה) — dis legomenon; ONLY other OT occurrence is Jdg 5:11 יְתַנּוּ צִדְקוֹת יְהוָה — "they recount/rehearse the righteous acts of the LORD" (CELEBRATIVE). LXX splits: Jdg 5:11 → διηγέομαι "recount"; Jdg 11:40 → θρηνεῖν "lament" — two different tonal renderings of one Hebrew verb. KEY: ל + בַת־יִפְתָּח — dative "TO/FOR the daughter" — ל of interest/benefit, not עַל of object-of-mourning. Contrast בכה על (weep over, dead-focused idiom) in 11:37-38 vs. תנה ל in 11:40 (recount to/for). KEY: מִיָּמִים יָמִימָה — "from days to days" distributive idiom = "yearly" (cf. Exo 13:10; 1 Sam 1:3; 1 Sam 2:19 — all CULTIC/PILGRIMAGE contexts)

=== Judges 5:11 — letannot control === מִקּוֹל מְחַצְצִים בֵּין מַשְׁאַבִּים שָׁם יְתַנּוּ צִדְקוֹת יְהוָה

KEY: יְתַנּוּ (Verb.Piel.Impf.3mp) — "they recount/rehearse" The ONLY other canonical Piel of תנה. Context = celebration of YHWH's saving deeds. LXX = διηγέομαι (recount, narrate).

================================================================ HEBREW PARSING — Vow Parallels (CONTROLS) ================================================================

=== Numbers 21:2 — Hormah vow-apodosis control === וַיִּדַּר יִשְׂרָאֵל נֶדֶר לַיהוָה וַיֹּאמַר אִם־נָתֹן תִּתֵּן אֶת־הָעָם הַזֶּה בְּיָדִי וְהַחֲרַמְתִּי אֶת־עָרֵיהֶם

Protasis: אִם + inf.abs. נָתֹן + Impf.2ms תִּתֵּן Apodosis: וְהַחֲרַמְתִּי (Hiphil.Perf.1s + weqatal חרם "utterly destroy") NOTE: SAME syntactic frame as Jdg 11:30-31 — but Num 21 uses SINGLE weqatal apodosis. Jephthah's is a TRIPLE weqatal chain.

=== 1 Samuel 1:11 — Hannah's vow (controlling parallel) === וַתִּדֹּר נֶדֶר וַתֹּאמַר יְהוָה צְבָאוֹת אִם־רָאֹה תִרְאֶה בָּעֳנִי אֲמָתֶךָ וּזְכַרְתַּנִי וְלֹא־תִשְׁכַּח אֶת־אֲמָתֶךָ וְנָתַתָּה לַאֲמָתְךָ זֶרַע אֲנָשִׁים וּנְתַתִּיו לַיהוָה כָּל־יְמֵי חַיָּיו וּמוֹרָה לֹא־יַעֲלֶה עַל־רֹאשׁוֹ

Apodosis: וּנְתַתִּיו לַיהוָה כָּל־יְמֵי חַיָּיו ו+Qal.Perf.1s+3ms נתן + ל + YHWH + "ALL THE DAYS OF HIS LIFE"

CRITICAL LEXICAL CONTRAST vs JEPHTHAH: - Hannah: וּנְתַתִּיו לַיהוָה (natan = "I will give/assign him") + כָּל־יְמֵי חַיָּיו ("all the days of his life" = LIFELONG) + Nazarite-separation marker ("no razor upon his head") - Jephthah: וְהָיָה לַיהוָה (hayah = "shall be to YHWH" — plain existence) + וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ עֹלָה ("and I will offer him up [as] burnt-offering") + NO life-duration clause + NO separation/consecration marker

The two vows employ DIFFERENT lexical registers: - Hannah's lexicon = NATAN + LIFELONG DURATION = dedication/lifelong service (cf. Levites wholly "given" natan-ed in Num 3:9; 8:16; 8:19) - Jephthah's lexicon = HAYAH LA-YHWH + Hiphil ALAH + OLAH-noun = SACRIFICIAL cultic collocation (cf. Gen 8:20; 22:2, 13; Lev 17:8 — Hiphil alah + olah is the technical burnt-offering phrase)

If Jephthah had intended Hannah-style lifelong-dedication, the available lexicon was natan + kol yemei chayyav. He did not use it. His chosen phrasing tracks the sacrificial register, not the dedication register.

=== 1 Samuel 1:28 — Hannah's fulfillment === וְגַם אָנֹכִי הִשְׁאִלְתִּהוּ לַיהוָה כָּל־הַיָּמִים אֲשֶׁר הָיָה הוּא שָׁאוּל לַיהוָה וַיִּשְׁתַּחוּ שָׁם לַיהוָה

KEY: הִשְׁאִלְתִּהוּ (Hiphil.Perf.1s + 3ms שׁאל) — "I have lent him" KEY: שָׁאוּל לַיהוָה — "he is lent/asked to YHWH" (pun on "Samuel/Shaul") The fulfillment makes Samuel LIVE and SERVE (cf. 1 Sam 2:11, 18 — the child ministered before YHWH), NOT be sacrificed.

=== Genesis 22:2 — Akedah === וַיֹּאמֶר קַח־נָא אֶת־בִּנְךָ אֶת־יְחִידְךָ אֲשֶׁר־אָהַבְתָּ אֶת־יִצְחָק וְלֶךְ־לְךָ אֶל־אֶרֶץ הַמֹּרִיָּה וְהַעֲלֵהוּ שָׁם לְעֹלָה עַל אַחַד הֶהָרִים אֲשֶׁר אֹמַר אֵלֶיךָ

LEXICAL NOTE: וְהַעֲלֵהוּ לְעֹלָה = SAME cultic collocation Hiphil עלה + עולה as Jephthah's וְהַעֲלִיתִהוּ עֹלָה in Jdg 11:31. Plus the Gen 22 use of יְחִידְךָ ("thine only [son]") is what Jdg 11:34 evokes with its unique use of יְחִידָה ("she [was his] only child"). Akedah = archetypal ARRESTED sacrifice (ram substitutes).

=== 2 Kings 3:27 — Mesha sacrifice CONTRAST === וַיִּקַּח אֶת־בְּנוֹ הַבְּכוֹר אֲשֶׁר־יִמְלֹךְ תַּחְתָּיו וַיַּעֲלֵהוּ עֹלָה עַל־הַחֹמָה

KEY: וַיַּעֲלֵהוּ עֹלָה (Verb.Hiphil.Wayq.3ms+3ms עלה + עלה "burnt-offering") "AND HE OFFERED HIM UP [AS] BURNT-OFFERING"

Diagnostic contrast with Jdg 11:39: when the OT narrator reports an actual case of human immolation in burnt-offering mode, the Hiphil עלה + עולה collocation IS USED. Jdg 11:39 has plain עשׂה (wayya'as) — no sacrificial vocabulary. The narrator's lexical restraint at Jdg 11:39 is conspicuous.

=== Jeremiah 7:31 — Divine Disavowal === וּבָנוּ בָּמוֹת הַתֹּפֶת אֲשֶׁר בְּגֵיא בֶן־הִנֹּם לִשְׂרֹף אֶת־בְּנֵיהֶם וְאֶת־בְּנֹתֵיהֶם בָּאֵשׁ אֲשֶׁר לֹא צִוִּיתִי וְלֹא עָלְתָה עַל־לִבִּי

KEY: לִשְׂרֹף אֶת־בְּנֵיהֶם וְאֶת־בְּנֹתֵיהֶם — "to burn their sons and daughters in the fire" (verb שׂרף "burn" used — sacrificial lexicon) KEY: אֲשֶׁר לֹא צִוִּיתִי (Piel.Perf.1s צוה "I did not command") KEY: וְלֹא עָלְתָה עַל־לִבִּי (Qal.Perf.3fs עלה) "neither came it up into my heart" — YHWH's express disavowal of human sacrifice as something He ever commanded or even conceived.

This forecloses ANY reading in which YHWH accepted/commanded Jephthah's daughter as a literal burnt offering. Regardless of whether Jephthah's vow was understood by himself as literal sacrifice, YHWH's verdict on such offerings in Jer 7:31 is unambiguous: not commanded, not conceived, not accepted.

=== Genesis 24:16 — Rebekah lo yada ish === וְהַנַּעֲרָ טֹבַת מַרְאֶה מְאֹד בְּתוּלָה וְאִישׁ לֹא יְדָעָהּ

KEY: בְּתוּלָה (Noun.fs.Abs) "a virgin" + וְאִישׁ לֹא יְדָעָהּ (WX-QATAL: ו + אִישׁ + Qal.Perf.3ms+3fs) "and a man had not known her" Rebekah is ALIVE; the lo-yada-ish formula describes her living sexual- status. Strongest parallel to Jdg 11:39 WX-QATAL.

=== 1 Kings 1:4 — Abishag lo yada === וְהַנַּעֲרָה יָפָה עַד־מְאֹד וַתְּהִי לַמֶּלֶךְ סֹכֶנֶת וַתְּשָׁרְתֵהוּ וְהַמֶּלֶךְ לֹא יְדָעָהּ

KEY: וְהַמֶּלֶךְ לֹא יְדָעָהּ — WX-QATAL of virginal attachment (Abishag ALIVE and serving David throughout his remaining lifetime). Structural parallel to Jdg 11:39 — BUT note: Abishag lives on and serves (vv. continuing into 1 Kings 2). The construction describes ONGOING living state, not a death-event aftermath.

=== Leviticus 27:2 — Vow-Valuation === דַּבֵּר אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְאָמַרְתָּ אֲלֵהֶם אִישׁ כִּי יַפְלִא נֶדֶר בְּעֶרְכְּךָ נְפָשֹׁת לַיהוָה

KEY: כִּי יַפְלִא נֶדֶר — "when [a man] makes a singular/special vow" Verb H6381 פלא (Hiphil "make special/extraordinary") + cognate accusative נֶדֶר KEY: בְּעֶרְכְּךָ נְפָשֹׁת לַיהוָה — "by thy ערך (valuation) persons [shall be] to YHWH" H6187 ערך is the priest-assigned monetary valuation; Lev 27:3-8 details the age/sex-graded tariff (female 5-20 yr = 10 shekels; 20-60 yr = 30 shekels). This is Torah's built-in off-ramp for person-vows.

=== Leviticus 27:28 — Cherem Law === אַךְ כָּל־חֵרֶם אֲשֶׁר יַחֲרִם אִישׁ לַיהוָה מִכָּל־אֲשֶׁר לוֹ מֵאָדָם וּבְהֵמָה וּמִשְּׂדֵה אֲחֻזָּתוֹ לֹא יִמָּכֵר וְלֹא יִגָּאֵל

KEY: כָּל־חֵרֶם אֲשֶׁר יַחֲרִם — CHEREM (H2764 noun + Hiphil H2763 verb) - IS NOT a regular neder - IS NOT redeemable (לֹא יִגָּאֵל) - Belongs to the category of judicial-ban (Josh 7 Achan; 1 Sam 15 Amalek)

=== Leviticus 27:29 — Cherem of Persons === כָּל־חֵרֶם אֲשֶׁר יָחֳרַם מִן־הָאָדָם לֹא יִפָּדֶה מוֹת יוּמָת

KEY: לֹא יִפָּדֶה — "shall not be redeemed" (Niphal H6299 פדה) KEY: מוֹת יוּמָת — "shall surely be put to death" (tautological inf.abs. + Hophal H4191 מות)

IMPORTANT: Lev 27:28-29 is CHEREM (judicial ban), NOT ordinary NEDER. Jephthah vowed a נֶדֶר, not a חֵרֶם. The non-redeemable-death-penalty provision of Lev 27:28-29 does NOT apply to private votive pledges; it applies to public judicial ban on Molech-worshipers (Lev 20:2-5), apostate cities (Deut 13:15), and Amalek/Canaan-type conquest-devotions. A private person-vow (neder) falls under Lev 27:1-8 — ALWAYS redeemable via the priest-administered ערך (valuation). The Targum Jonathan gloss on Jdg 11:39 (via Clarke 28231) takes exactly this position: Jephthah's fault lay in NOT CONSULTING PHINEHAS, who would have applied Lev 27:1-8 redemption.

=== Numbers 30:2 — Vow-Payment Absolutism + Num 30:3-5 Father's Annulment === Num 30:2: If a man vow a vow unto the LORD... he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth. Num 30:3-5: A woman's vow, made "in her father's house in her youth," is SUBJECT to her father's annulment on the day he hears it. If he disallows it, "the LORD shall forgive her, because her father disallowed her" (v. 5).

NOTE: This provides a THIRD off-ramp Jephthah could have invoked AS FATHER. Yet the narrator records NO annulment and NO priest-consultation. Num 30 applies to the class of vow (woman in father's house) of which the daughter is subject — BUT Jephthah is the VOWER, not the daughter. His own oath falls under Num 30:2 (a man's vow) which he interprets as irrevocable.

=== Deuteronomy 12:31 — Categorical Prohibition === לֹא־תַעֲשֶׂה כֵן לַיהוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ כִּי כָּל־תּוֹעֲבַת יְהוָה אֲשֶׁר שָׂנֵא עָשׂוּ לֵאלֹהֵיהֶם כִּי גַם אֶת־בְּנֵיהֶם וְאֶת־בְּנֹתֵיהֶם יִשְׂרְפוּ בָאֵשׁ לֵאלֹהֵיהֶם

KEY: לֹא־תַעֲשֶׂה כֵן לַיהוָה — "THOU SHALT NOT DO SO UNTO YHWH" Explicit prohibition against applying Canaanite-style child immolation to YHWH-worship. Jephthah's act, on the literal reading, would be this exact prohibited act.

=== Deuteronomy 18:10 — Pass-Through-Fire Prohibition === לֹא־יִמָּצֵא בְךָ מַעֲבִיר בְּנוֹ־וּבִתּוֹ בָּאֵשׁ

KEY: מַעֲבִיר (Hiphil.Ptcp.ms עבר) בְּנוֹ־וּבִתּוֹ בָּאֵשׁ "one causing his son or his daughter to pass through the fire" Categorical prohibition, no exception for private vow-motivated use.

docs.title: Nave's Entries
docs.text:
================================================================ NAVE'S TOPICAL ENTRIES (full) Source: naves_db.py --topic ================================================================

=== JEPHTHAH === (A judge (leader, hero) of Israel) - Illegitimate, and therefore not entitled to inherit his father's property JDG 11:1, 2 - Escapes the violence of his half-brothers; lives in the land of Tob JDG 11:3 - Recalled from the land of Tob by the elders of Gilead JDG 11:5 - Made captain of the army JDG 11:5-11 - Made head of the land of Gilead JDG 11:7-11 - His message to the king of the Ammonites JDG 11:12-28 - Leads the army of Israel against the Ammonites JDG 11:29-33 - His rash vow concerning his daughter JDG 11:31, 34-40 - Falsely accused by the Ephraimites JDG 12:1 - Leads the army of the Gileadites against the Ephraimites JDG 12:4 - Leads Israel for six years, dies, and is buried in Gilead JDG 12:7 - Faith of HEB 11:32

NOTE: Nave's entry classifies 11:31, 34-40 explicitly under "rash vow" — an interpretive editorial choice reflecting the literal-sacrifice reading. This is Nave's interpretation, not Scripture's verdict.

=== VOWS === - Mosaic laws concerning LEV 23:37, 38; NUM 29:39 - Must be voluntary, see below - Must be performed, see below - Estimation of the redemption price of things offered in vows, to be made by the priest, according to age and sex of the person making the offering LEV 27:1-13 - The redemption price of the offering of real estate, to be valued by the priest LEV 27:14, 15; of a field LEV 27:16-25 - Edible things offered in, to be eaten the same day they were offered LEV 7:16-18 - Things offered in, to be brought to the tabernacle or temple DEU 12:6, 11, 17, 18, 26 - Belonged to the priests NUM 18:14

RASH VOWS: - By Jephthah, in consecration of his daughter as a sacrifice, if his campaign against the Ammonites were successful JDG 11:29-40 - The Israelites, to destroy the Benjamites JDG 21:5, 6; with 20:21

  • Things forbidden to be offered in: Receipts of the whore and the price of a "dog" (male prostitute of a pagan shrine) DEU 23:18
  • A minor, of himself MRK 7:11-13

UNCLASSIFIED SCRIPTURES RELATING TO: GEN 28:20, 21; 31:13; LEV 5:4-13; 22:18-25; NUM 15:2-16; 30:1-16; DEU 23:18, 21-23; JOB 22:27; PSA 22:25; 50:14, 15; 56:12; 61:5, 8; 65:1; 66:13, 14; 76:11; 116:14-19; PRO 20:25; ECC 5:4-6; JON 2:9; NAM 1:15

INSTANCES OF: - Of Jacob: GEN 28:20-22 - Of the mother of Micah, in the dedication of silver for the making of an idol: JDG 17:2, 3 - Of Hannah, to consecrate to the Lord the child for which she prayed: 1SA 1:11; with 1:27, 28 - Of Elkanah: 1SA 1:21 - Of Absalom: 2SA 15:7, 8 - Of Job, not to entertain thoughts of fornication: JOB 31:1 - Of David: PSA 132:2 - Of Ananias and Sapphira, in the dedication of the proceeds of the sale of their land: ACT 5:1-11 - Of the Jews, to kill Paul: ACT 23:12-15 - Of Jephthah, and of the Israelites (see RASH VOWS, above)

See NAZARITE.

=== DEDICATION === - Law concerning dedicated things LEV 27; NUM 18:14; 1CH 26:26, 27 - Must be without blemish LEV 22:18-23; MAL 1:14 - Not redeemable LEV 27:28, 29 - Offering must be voluntary LEV 1:3; 22:19 - See OFFERINGS, VOWS - Of the tabernacle NUM 7 - Solomon's temple 1KI 8; 2CH 7:5 - Second temple EZR 6:16, 17 - Of the wall of Jerusalem NEH 12:27-43 - Of houses DEU 20:5 - Of Samuel by his mother 1SA 1:11, 22

OF SELF: - See CONSECRATION - For instances of liberality in dedicated things, see LIBERALITY - See FEAST OF DEDICATION JHN 10:22; 1KI 8:65; 2CH 7:8-10

IMPORTANT: Nave's entry for DEDICATION cross-references LEV 27:28, 29 as "Not redeemable" — which the user should note refers to the CHEREM category (Lev 27:28-29), NOT the ordinary neder category (Lev 27:1-8). The two are legally distinct. Jephthah vowed a neder, not a cherem.

=== VIRGIN === - Proofs of DEU 22:13-21 - Dowry of EXO 22:17 - Character of, to be protected DEU 22:17-21, 23, 24 - Betrothal of, a quasi-marriage DEU 22:23, 24 - Distinguishing apparel of 2SA 13:18 - A priest could marry only a LEV 21:14 - Mourn in the temple LAM 1:4; 2:10 - Virginity of, bewailed JDG 11:37-39 - Parable of the wise and foolish MAT 25:1-13 - Mother of Jesus was a ISA 7:14; MAT 1:23; LUK 1:27 - Advised by Paul not to marry 1CO 7

FIGURATIVE: - Of the body of Christ (the ekklesia) ISA 62:5; JER 14:17; 31:4, 13; 2CO 11:2 - Of personal purity 1CO 7:25, 37; REV 14:4

NOTE: Nave's heading "Virginity of, bewailed JDG 11:37-39" implicitly reads Jdg 11:37-39 as mourning-of-virginity — consistent with the living- dedication reading (the daughter mourns an enduring STATE, not impending death).

=== OATH === (A solemn qualification) - Used in solemnizing covenants. Numerous individual instances.

Key items for this study: - The elders of Gilead confirm their pledge to Jephthah by [oath] JDG 11:10 (establishes Jephthah's juridical legitimacy as judge) - The Israelites swear in Mizpeh JDG 21:5 (rash-oath Benjamite parallel)

NT on oath-ethics: - MAT 5:33-37 (Jesus — swear not at all) - JAS 5:12 (James — let yea be yea)

UNCLASSIFIED: EXO 20:7; 22:10, 11; LEV 6:2-5; 19:12; NUM 5:19-24; DEU 5:11; 6:13; 10:20; 1KI 8:31, 32; PSA 15:1, 2, 4; ECC 8:2; ISA 48:1; JER 4:2; 5:2, 7; 7:8, 9; 12:16; DAN 9:11; 12:7; HOS 4:15; MAT 23:18-22; 26:63; MRK 14:71; ACT 23:12-14; 2CO 1:23; GAL 1:20; HEB 6:16; REV 10:5, 6.

=== NAZARITE === - Law concerning NUM 6:1-21; JDG 13:5 - Character of LAM 4:7; AMO 2:11, 12

INSTANCES OF: - Samson JDG 13:5, 7; 16:17 - Samuel 1SA 1:11 - Rechabites JER 35 - John the Baptist MAT 11:18; LUK 1:15; 7:33

NOTE: The SAMUEL entry here establishes Samuel as a Nazarite by his mother's vow (1 Sam 1:11), confirming that the structural parallel to Hannah's vow = living consecration with Nazarite-style markers.

=== MOLECH === - Also called MOLOCH and MILCOM - An idol of the Ammonites ACT 7:43 - Worshiped by the wives of Solomon, and by Solomon 1KI 11:1-8 - Children sacrificed to: 2KI 23:10; JER 32:35; 2KI 16:3; 21:6; 2CH 28:3; ISA 57:5; JER 7:31; EZK 16:20, 21; 20:26, 31; 23:37, 39 - See LEV 18:21; 20:2-5

NOTE: Molech was the idol of AMMON specifically (Act 7:43; 1 Kgs 11:5 "Milcom the abomination of the Ammonites"). Jephthah was fighting Ammon (Jdg 11). If Jephthah had offered his daughter as a burnt offering, he would have been replicating the very abomination of the god of the enemy he had just defeated — a grotesque inversion that exacerbates the theological problem.

=== CONSECRATION === - Of Aaron: See AARON - Of priests: See PRIESTS - Of the altar: See ALTAR - Of the temple: See TEMPLE, DEDICATION OF - See also OFFERINGS

PERSONAL: PSA 51:17; MAT 13:44-46; ROM 6:13, 16, 19; 12:1; 2CO 8:5

CONDITIONAL: GEN 28:20-22; 2SA 15:7, 8 See DEDICATION, OFFERINGS.

INSTANCES OF: - Cain and Abel GEN 4:4-7 - Abraham, of Isaac GEN 22:9-12 [Akedah — arrested sacrifice] - Jephthah, of his daughter JDG 11:30, 31, 34-40 [contested] - Hannah, of Samuel 1SA 1:11, 24-28 [lifelong service] - David consecrates the water obtained by his valiant warriors 2SA 23:16 - Zichri, of himself 2CH 17:16

NOTE: Nave's editor groups Jephthah's daughter under CONSECRATION (not under SACRIFICE or OFFERING), placing her alongside Isaac (arrested) and Samuel (lifelong served). The editorial grouping suggests the daughter's case is MORE AT HOME in the consecration-dedication semantic domain than in the human-sacrifice domain.

=== HUMAN SACRIFICE === See OFFERING, HUMAN [redirect — not a separately listed entry]

=== BURNT OFFERING === See OFFERINGS, BURNT [redirect]

================================================================ CROSS-THEMATIC SYNTHESIS (for Focus Areas) ================================================================

Focus Area 1-3 (Grammar of vow) — Nave's JEPHTHAH + VOWS entries supply the verse list (Jdg 11:1-40; 12:1-7; Heb 11:32 for the man; Lev 27, Num 30, Deut 12, Ecc 5 for the legal framework; Gen 28:20, Num 21:2, 1 Sam 1:11, 2 Sam 15:7 for the vow-parallels).

Focus Area 4-5 (bethulim, tanah) — Nave's VIRGIN entry places "Virginity of, bewailed JDG 11:37-39" in the living-women's virginity category alongside Deut 22:13-21, Lev 21:14, Lam 1:4; 2:10 (virgins mourning in temple) — all LIVING-status references.

Focus Area 6 (neder vs cherem, redemption) — Nave's DEDICATION entry distinguishes Lev 27:1-25 redemption-mechanisms from Lev 27:28-29 cherem non-redemption. The vow-of-persons regulation (Lev 27:1-8) is under DEDICATION, not under cherem. Lev 27:1-13 places Jephthah's daughter-vow in the redeemable category by default.

Focus Area 7 (human-sacrifice prohibition) — Nave's MOLECH entry gathers the full prohibition-and-condemnation corpus: Lev 18:21; 20:2-5; 2 Kgs 23:10; Jer 32:35; 7:31; 2 Kgs 16:3; 21:6; Isa 57:5; Ezk 16:20, 21; 20:26, 31; 23:37, 39. The act Jephthah's literal reading would commit is explicitly the act of Molech worship that the Ammonites (his enemy) practiced.

Focus Area 8 (Hannah parallel) — Nave's NAZARITE + VOWS (Instances) + DEDICATION entries all cross-reference 1 Sam 1:11 as the structural dedication parallel.

Focus Area 9 (Spirit + Judges) — Nave's JEPHTHAH entry under "Leads the army of Israel against the Ammonites JDG 11:29-33" scopes the Spirit- inhabitation to the military campaign, not to the vow episode.

Focus Area 10 (Heb 11:32) — Nave's JEPHTHAH entry closes with "Faith of HEB 11:32" — ONE line. The faith-commendation is unadorned and does not specify vow or victory.

docs.title: Cross-Testament Parallels
docs.text:
================================================================ CROSS-TESTAMENT PARALLELS + PARALLEL-VOW ANALYSIS Source: cross_testament_parallels_v2.py (attempted — see note); hand- compiled from library entries + verse retrieval ================================================================

NOTE ON TOOL FAILURE: cross_testament_parallels_v2.py returned "Verse not found" for every book-code variant of Jdg 11:31 and Jdg 11:39 attempted: - "JDG 11:31" — not found - "Judg 11:31" — not found - "Judges 11:31" — not found - "JDG 11:39" — not found (both hybrid-ot and hybrid-nt) - "Judges 11:31" --find-nt / --find-ot — not found

The indexed parallels database appears not to cover these particular verses (possibly a gap in the v2 index build). This has been noted to the analysis agent; the parallel-verse data below is compiled manually from library entries, verse retrieval, and concept_context.py output (which worked).

================================================================ PARALLEL VOW CONSTRUCTIONS (protasis im + weqatal apodosis) ================================================================

Passage Vower Protasis Apodosis Outcome Gen 28:20-22 Jacob im yihye elohim immadi wehayah YHWH li leelohim + Tithe paid; Jacob lived asser aasserennu Num 21:2 Israel im naton titten et ha'am wehacharamti et areihem Cities devoted (cherem) — hazzeh beyadi (SINGLE weqatal apodosis) JUDICIAL BAN, not sacrifice Jdg 11:30-31 Jephthah im naton titten et bnei wehayah hayotze... wehayah CONTESTED ammon beyadi laYHWH wehaalitihu olah (TRIPLE weqatal apodosis) 1 Sam 1:11 Hannah im ra'oh tireh... wezechartani... unetattiw laYHWH kol yemei Samuel LIVED — lifelong venatatta... zera anashim chayyav + razor prohibition sanctuary service 2 Sam 15:7-8 Absalom im ya'shiv yeshiveni YHWH veavadti et YHWH Absalom pretended — plot cover yerushalaim Num 21:2 Israel (same as above) (above)

OBSERVATIONS: 1. ALL five vow constructions follow the SAME grammatical template (im + inf.abs.+impf. protasis / weqatal apodosis). 2. In four out of five (Jacob, Hannah, Absalom, Israel/Num 21), the default conjunctive-weqatal force is uncontroversial. The apodosis expresses one reward or one action (or a coordinated sequence). 3. Num 21:2 uses חרם (cherem) not נדר (neder)-plus-olah. That vow's apodosis (wehacharamti) is a PUBLIC JUDICIAL BAN, lawful in conquest against a Canaanite oppressor (Arad). It is NOT a private person-vow like Jephthah's. 4. Hannah (1 Sam 1:11) is the lexically closest dedication-model: SAME "laYHWH" dative frame, PLUS explicit "all the days of his life" + separation-marker. Jephthah omits both. 5. The disjunctive-waw reading of Jdg 11:31, if genuinely admissible, would be a UNIQUE case — no other OT vow demonstrably uses disjunctive waw in the apodosis chain.

================================================================ PARALLEL "LO YADA ISH" CONSTRUCTIONS (virginity formula) ================================================================

Passage Subject Construction Living/dead Gen 19:8 Lot's daughters "which have not known man" Living Gen 24:16 Rebekah bethulah + WX-QATAL ve'ish lo yeda'ah Living Num 31:17 Midianite women sexually-experienced marked for death (killed) Num 31:18 Midianite virgin girls "that have not known..." — SPARED Living Num 31:35 32,000 virgin count "women that had not known man" Living Jdg 11:39 Jephthah's daughter WX-QATAL ve-hi lo yad'ah ish DISPUTED Jdg 19:25 Gibeah concubine (sexually abused — "knew her") Dead from abuse Jdg 21:11-12 Jabesh-Gilead virgins "known no man by lying with male" Living 1 Kgs 1:4 Abishag WX-QATAL ve-ha-melek lo yeda'ah Living

PATTERN: Every other OT "lo yada ish / lo yeda'ah" applies to a LIVING woman whose sexual status matters for ongoing social/marital context (priestly marriage, military captivity, royal chastity). NONE describes a deceased woman's past virginity. The narrator's choice of the WX-QATAL + lo-yada-ish formula at Jdg 11:39 places the daughter in the semantic category of LIVING virginal chastity, not of cut-short-life mourning.

================================================================ PARALLEL DEDICATION/CONSECRATION OF PERSONS TO YHWH ================================================================

Passage Dedicant Lexical markers Living? Exo 13:2 All firstborn qaddesh (consecrate) + kol peter rechem Living Lev 27:1-8 Any person-vow PRIEST-redeemed by 'erek valuation Living Num 3:9, 8:16 Levites netunim (doubled) laYHWH Living Num 6:1-21 Nazarite (general) hizzir (separate) + le + YHWH Living Jdg 13:5-7 Samson (Nazarite) Nazir elohim + razor prohibition Living 1 Sam 1:11, 22, 28 Samuel natan laYHWH + kol yemei chayyav Living 1 Sam 2:11,18 Samuel's fulfillment "ministered to YHWH" — "girded with ephod" Living Luke 2:36-37 Anna (prophetess widow) "departed not from the temple" served Living nystiaìais "with fastings" etc. 1 Cor 7:34 Unmarried woman "holy both in body and in spirit" Living Rev 14:4 144,000 "virgins" "not defiled with women" (figurative) Living

PATTERN: Every unambiguous case of person-dedication in Scripture results in LIVING SERVICE — the Nazarite, Samuel, Levite, Anna, Paul's virgin all serve YHWH or His sanctuary while alive. There is NO canonical precedent for dedication-by-immolation of a human being to YHWH (except the Akedah, which is EXPLICITLY a test arrested by divine intervention with ram-substitution).

================================================================ CONCEPT-CONTEXT FINDINGS (summary of concept_context.py runs) ================================================================

Jdg 11:31 — Concepts found: EXODUS (yatsa), REPENTANCE (shub), SACRIFICE (olah). Same-chapter matches (8): Jdg 11:3, 8, 9, 13, 34, 35, 36, 39 — all Jephthah vow-narrative internal echoes. Same-book matches (10): Jdg 6:18, 13:16, 13:19, 13:23, 15:19 — Gideon and Manoah parallels. Notably, Jdg 13:16-23 pairs burnt-offering with ANGEL- OF-YHWH intervention, echoing Akedah-style arrested-sacrifice pattern (the angel tells Manoah to offer a burnt offering to YHWH, not himself/his son).

Jdg 11:39 — Concepts found: TELOS (qets), REPENTANCE (shub), FULFILLMENT (asah), LAW (choq). Same-chapter matches include Jdg 11:10 (elders' pledge to Jephthah), 11:13, 11:27 ("the LORD the Judge"), 11:31, 11:35, 11:36. Same-book matches: Jdg 17:3-4 Micah's mother's silver-dedication, Jdg 2:17 (apostasy refrain), Jdg 21:15, 21:23 (repenting of Benjamin-vow). The cluster of "vow/dedication + repent + statute" markers connects Jephthah's episode to the Judges-wide pattern of rash oaths (Jdg 21) and irregular cultic practice (Micah's silver — Jdg 17 private cult).

1 Sam 1:11 (Hannah) — Concepts: PROPHET (ra'ah/naba), LIFE (chay). Same- chapter matches: 1 Sam 1:10, 15, 22, 26 (her weeping, her answer to Eli, her bringing Samuel to the temple, her oath "as thy soul liveth"). The concept-context algorithm tagged Hannah's vow under the LIFE field, not SACRIFICE — because Hannah's vow-verb is natan + "all the days of his LIFE," not olah. This is exactly the lexical register Jephthah's vow lacks.

Lev 27:2 — Concepts found: LIFE (nefesh — "persons"). Semantic tag confirms the law governs the VOWING of LIVING PERSONS, regulated by priest-valuation rather than by immolation. Same-book matches limit to Leviticus food-law verses (Lev 11 series) — not theologically relevant; demonstrates that the concept_context semantic-classifier did not pick up any "sacrifice" tag for Lev 27:2. The vow-of-persons regulation is about PERSONS LIVING, not persons dying.

================================================================ NOTE ON LXX TEXT COMPARISON (see grammar-reference/greek/lxx-disjunctive-waw-vows.md) ================================================================

LXX Jdg 11:31: A-text: καὶ ἔσται τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ ἀνοίσω αὐτὸν ὁλοκαύτωμα ("and it shall be to the LORD and I will offer him up [as] whole-burnt-offering") → contested waw rendered with KAI (conjunctive) B-text: καὶ ἔσται τῷ κυρίῳ· ἀνοίσω αὐτὸν ὁλοκαύτωμα ("and it shall be to the LORD; I will offer him up...") → asyndeton (no conjunction)

Neither recension uses ἤ ("or") or εἰ μή ("unless"). Both Greek particles were available to the translators (used elsewhere for genuine disjunction, e.g., Lev 5:1 ἤ...ἤ for אוֹ... אוֹ). Their ABSENCE at Jdg 11:31 is substantive negative evidence against the disjunctive-waw reading. The earliest pre- Christian Jewish readers did NOT see disjunction here.

LXX Jdg 5:11: διηγέομαι (G1334) "recount" LXX Jdg 11:40: θρηνεῖν (G2354) "lament"

The LXX translators split the dis legomenon תנה into two Greek verbs, one celebratory and one mournful. The LXX Jdg 11:40 choice of θρηνεῖν reflects the sacrificial-death reading of the narrative. But LXX Jdg 5:11 confirms that the Hebrew root itself in Piel carries "recount/celebrate" force.

================================================================ TEXTUAL VARIANT — Heb 11:32 N1904 vs TR ================================================================

N1904: 22 words. Includes λέγω (G3004), ἐπιλείψει (G1952), χρόνος (G5550), με (G1473), διηγούμενον (G1334), προφητῶν (G4396). TR: 20 words. Omits subject-pronoun; minor orthography shifts (Δαβίδ vs Δαυείδ).

No variant affects the inclusion of Ἰεφθάε or the structure of the nine- aorist-verb list in vv. 33-34. Both texts place Jephthah in the faith-roll.

docs.title: Strong's Lookups
docs.text:
================================================================ STRONG'S LOOKUPS Source: library entries (bible-studies/word-studies/) ================================================================

All critical Strong's numbers for this study HAVE LIBRARY ENTRIES; no fresh --lookup queries were needed. Library file paths listed below for reference.

Strong's Lemma Library file Used at H5087 nadar H5087-nadar.md Jdg 11:30 (verb) H5088 neder H5088-neder.md Jdg 11:30, 39 (noun) H5927 'alah H5927-alah.md Jdg 11:31 Hiphil H5930 'olah H5930-olah.md Jdg 11:31 burnt-off. H2076 zabach H2076-zabach.md ABSENT at Jdg 11:39 H2763 charam H2763-charam.md Lev 27:28-29 H2764 cherem H2764-cherem.md Lev 27:28 H1330 bethulah H1330-bthuwlah.md Jdg 11 implicit H1331 bethulim H1331-bthuwliym.md Jdg 11:37-38 H3045 yada' H3045-yada.md Jdg 11:39 H5144 nazar H5144-nazar.md Num 6 parallel H8567 tanah H8567-tanah.md Jdg 11:40 / Jdg 5:11 H2403 chattaah H2403-chattaah.md Lev 5 atonement H7311 rum H7311-ruwm.md semantic field H8199 shaphat H8199-shaphat.md Jdg 12:7 (Jephthah) H3173 yachiyd H3173-yachiyd.md Jdg 11:34 / Gen 22:2 H6187 'erek H6187-erek.md Lev 27:2-8 (redemption) H6942 qadash H6942-qadash.md dedication semantics H5414 natan H5414-nathan.md 1 Sam 1:11 Hannah H3318 yatsa H3318-yatsa.md Jdg 11:31 hayyotze

================================================================ KEY LEXICAL FINDINGS FROM LIBRARY ENTRIES ================================================================

H3318 yatsa hayyotze participle referent-scope (Jdg 11:31): - Out of 51 Qal ms absolute participle forms in OT, approx distribution: - Human individual (~14): named person exits a place - Human collective (~20): military/civic "going out" - Offspring from loins (4): Gen 15:4, Gen 17:6, 1 Ki 8:19, 2 Chr 6:9 - YHWH/divine agent (~5): Isa 26:21 etc. - Inanimate/natural (~15): rivers, lightning, word-from-mouth - Animal subject: ZERO direct cases - Specifically, "hayyotze + lo + lo-qeratiti" / "coming out to meet me" + "doors of my house" is UNIQUE to Jdg 11:31 in the OT. Closest idiomatic neighbors (Num 27:17 "goeth out and cometh in"; 1 Ki 3:7; Prov 7:15) invariably describe HUMAN leadership/social movement. - The formulaic frame SKEWS strongly toward a HUMAN referent (per the library entry): "The grammatical pattern yotze + liqrati ('coming out to meet me') is itself strongly human — meeting (qara / liqrat) is a social act performed by persons, not animals, across its ~100 OT occurrences." - COUNTER-POINT (from the Josephus paraphrase in Ant. 5.7.10): "what living creature soever should first meet him" — Josephus takes the participle as GENERIC creature, not specifically human. The Hebrew pattern + Gileadite house-courtyard setting (livestock sometimes kept inside family compounds) could admit a wider referent, but the GRAMMATICAL DEFAULT is human.

H3173 yachiyd (Jdg 11:34): - 12 OT occurrences — LXX splits as ἀγαπητός (7x, in sacrifice/mourning frames — Gen 22:2, 12, 16 Isaac; Jdg 11:34; Jer 6:26; Amos 8:10; Zech 12:10) and μονογενής (4x, in the Psalmist's "one irreplaceable life" frame). - Jdg 11:34 is the ONLY OT verse where LXX uses BOTH ἀγαπητή AND μονογενής (paired — "only [and] beloved") — importing the full Akedah-echo vocabulary into the Jephthah narrative. - 42% of yachid occurrences cluster in "only-child-threatened-with- sacrifice" contexts: Gen 22 (Isaac averted), Jdg 11 (Jephthah's daughter — contested), Zech 12:10 (Messianic pierced one — arrested by resurrection).

H6187 'erek (Lev 27:2-8 redemption valuation): - 35 OT tokens in 29 verses; 20 of those in Leviticus (Lev 27 redemption tariff + Lev 5 trespass-offering). - The canonical off-ramp: "Lev 27:1-8 establishes the canonical Torah mechanism for discharging a vow that has devoted a person to YHWH: (1) the vow is binding, (2) but the person is not to be literally offered; instead the priest assigns a silver valuation, (3) the poverty clause scales the tariff, (4) only objects placed under cherem are non-redeemable. The category EXPLICITLY CONTEMPLATES THE SCENARIO OF JDG 11 — a hastily-sworn person-vow. Torah's answer is: pay the priestly valuation. The silver discharges the vow; the person goes home."

H5414 natan vs H5927 Hiphil 'alah (vow-verb contrast): - natan laYHWH + kol yemei chayyav = Hannah/Levite LIVING-SERVICE dedication - Hiphil 'alah + 'olah (burnt offering) = technical cultic immolation - Jdg 11:31 uses HAYAH laYHWH (plain) + Hiphil 'alah + 'olah (technical sacrificial collocation). Neither Hannah-pattern (natan) nor Lev 27:2 person-vow-pattern (yaphli neder + 'erek) is lexically present.

H1331 bethulim (abstract virginity-state): - 10 OT tokens in 9 verses. Every occurrence describes a LIVING woman's sexual status (Lev 21:13 priestly marriage; Deut 22:14-20 bride-defamation trial; Ezek 23:3, 8 figurative of Israel's youth; Jdg 11:37-38 Jephthah's daughter) — NONE describes a deceased woman. The abstract plural signals "state of virginity as a CONDITION," the condition Jephthah's daughter mourns for TWO MONTHS.

H8567 tanah (dis legomenon): - Only Jdg 5:11 (Piel impf. "they shall recount") and Jdg 11:40 (Piel inf. const.) — confined to Judges. - LXX splits: Jdg 5:11 → διηγέομαι (recount); Jdg 11:40 → θρηνεῖν (lament). - The 5:11 control is CELEBRATIVE — "they shall rehearse the righteous acts of the LORD." If Jdg 5:11 governs Jdg 11:40, the annual rite is celebrative commemoration, not funeral lament. - Syntactic frame in 11:40: ל (of interest) + bat-Yiftach, NOT עַל (of object-of-mourning). One mourns עַל someone; one recounts ל someone.

H2076 zabach: - 127 OT verses, 149 tokens; the standard sacrifice-slaughter verb. - CONSPICUOUS ABSENCE from Jdg 11:39. The narrator does not say "wayyizbahch et bitto" ("and he sacrificed his daughter"). Instead: wayya'as (plain "do/make") — a lexical restraint that cannot be read as the equivalent of a sacrifice-verb elsewhere.

H2763 charam / H2764 cherem: - The PUBLIC JUDICIAL BAN category (Achan, Amalek, Arad-cities). - Lev 27:28-29 explicitly restricts non-redemption + death-penalty to cherem cases. Lev 27:1-8 covers ordinary neder — ALL redeemable. - Num 21:2 Israel's vow uses charam (not olah); it is a LAWFUL public judicial ban. NOT a precedent for private person-immolation. - Jephthah's vow is נֶדֶר (Jdg 11:30: "vayyiddar Yiphtach NEDER la-YHWH"), NOT חֵרֶם. So Lev 27:28-29 does not apply; Lev 27:1-8 does.

docs.title: Topics Research — The Man of Sin (2 Thess 2:3-4, 8-12)
Question
docs.text: Who is "the man of sin" / "son of perdition" / "that Wicked" / "the lawless one" in 2 Thess 2:3-12?

docs.title: Naves Topic Index (summary — full entries retrieved to raw-data/naves-entries.txt)
ANTICHRIST
docs.text:
Nave's ANTICHRIST entry lists the figure under these biblical coordinates:

  • Warnings by Christ himself — MAT 24:5, 23, 24, 26; MRK 13:6, 21, 22; LUK 21:8 ("many shall come in my name… I am Christ")
  • Paul's catalogue (the exact 2 Thess 2 pericope) — 2 TH 2:3-12 (all twelve verses explicitly indexed under ANTICHRIST)
  • Johannine vocabulary — 1 JN 2:18 ("antichrist shall come, even now are there many antichrists"); 1 JN 2:22 ("he is antichrist, that denieth the Father and the Son"); 1 JN 4:3 ("this is that spirit of antichrist"); 2 JN 1:7 ("This is a deceiver and an antichrist")
  • Destruction of the figure in Revelation — REV 19:20 (beast + false prophet cast into lake of fire); REV 20:10, 15

Nave's ANTICHRIST therefore integrates three lexical streams the biblical text keeps distinct: Paul's anthrōpos tēs anomias / huios tēs apōleias / ho anomos; John's antichristos; and Revelation's thērion. The topic is a synthetic category; Paul himself does NOT use G500 antichristos.

docs.title: APOSTASY
docs.text:
The Nave's APOSTASY entry (~60 verses) divides into these sub-registers:

OT apostasy lexicon: - DEU 13:13 (sons of Belial, "let us go and serve other gods"); DEU 32:15 (Jeshurun waxed fat); ISA 1:28 (forsakers of the LORD shall be consumed); ISA 65:11-16; JER 17:5-6 ("cursed be the man that trusteth in man"); EZK 3:20; 18:24, 26; 33:12, 13, 18; 1 CH 28:9; ZEP 1:4-6

Gospels — Olivet-discourse apostasy prediction: - MAT 13:20-21 (stony-ground hearers "offended" in tribulation); MAT 24:9-12 ("many shall be offended… iniquity shall abound, love of many wax cold"); LUK 8:13 ("in time of temptation fall away")

Epistles — late apostasy warning chain (bearing directly on 2 Thess 2:3): - 1 CO 9:27 (Paul fearing self-reproof); 1 TI 1:19-20 (Hymenaeus and Alexander — faith shipwrecked); 1 TI 4:1-3 ("the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils; speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron; forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats"); 2 TI 3:1-9 (perilous times shall come, men shall be lovers of their own selves, having a form of godliness but denying the power); 2 TI 4:3-4 (not endure sound doctrine, turn from truth, turn to fables); 2 TI 4:10 (Demas forsook Paul); HEB 3:12 ("take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God"); HEB 6:4-8 (those who fall away); HEB 10:25-31, 39 (forsaking the assembling, willful sin); 2 TH 2:3 ("except there come a falling away first"); 2 TH 2:11-12 (God sends strong delusion); 2 PE 2:1-22 (false prophets, false teachers, damnable heresies); 2 PE 3:17 (being led away with the error of the wicked); Jude 1:4-6

docs.title: BLASPHEMY
docs.text: Sub-entry "blasphemy in eschatological figures": - DAN 7:25 (speaks great words against the most High) - DAN 11:36-37 (speaks marvellous things against the God of gods) - 2 TH 2:4 (exalts himself above all that is called God) - 2 TI 3:2 (men shall be blasphemers) - HEB 10:29 (trod underfoot the Son of God, done despite to the Spirit) - REV 13:1 (seven heads, the name of blasphemy); REV 13:5-6 (a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies… opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, against his name, and his tabernacle, and them that dwell in heaven) - REV 16:9, 11, 21; REV 17:3 (beast full of names of blasphemy) - MAT 12:31-32 (blasphemy against the Holy Ghost)

docs.title: TEMPLE
docs.text:
Nave's TEMPLE topic spans the literal and figurative axes both of which are decisive for 2 Thess 2:4:

Pauline "temple of God = believers/church": - 1 CO 3:16-17 ("ye are the temple of God… the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are") - 1 CO 6:19 ("your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost") - 2 CO 6:16 ("ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them") - EPH 2:20-22 ("built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone; in whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord: in whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit") - 1 PE 2:5, 9 ("ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house"; "a royal priesthood, an holy nation") - HEB 3:6 ("whose house are we")

Prophetic / eschatological temple: - 2 TH 2:4 (sits in the naos of God) - REV 11:1 (measure the naos) - DAN 8:11-15 (sanctuary cast down, the daily taken away) - DAN 8:13, 14 (the 2300 evenings-mornings; sanctuary cleansed)

Christ's body and literal standing temple: - JHN 2:19 ("destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up") - MAT 24:1-2, 15 (standing temple, abomination in the holy place)

docs.title: SANCTUARY
docs.text:
• EXO 25:8 (let them make me a sanctuary that I may dwell among them)
  • EZK 11:16 ("I will be to them as a little sanctuary in the countries where they shall come")
  • LAM 2:7, 20; EZK 42:20 (holy/profane distinction)
  • HEB 8:2, 5; HEB 9:2 (the true tabernacle pitched by the Lord, not man)

docs.title: WORSHIP
docs.text: Exclusive-worship register — bears on 2 Thess 2:4 "above all that is worshipped" (sebasma): - EXO 20:3; DEU 5:7; 6:13 (thou shalt worship the LORD thy God, and him only shalt thou serve) - MAT 4:10; LUK 4:8 (Christ's temptation — same commandment) - ACT 10:26 (Peter rejects worship from Cornelius) - ACT 14:15 (Paul and Barnabas reject worship at Lystra) - COL 2:18 (voluntary humility and worshipping of angels — proscribed) - REV 19:10; 22:8-9 (John rebuked for worshipping an angel)

docs.title: WICKED (THE WICKED) / WICKED (PEOPLE)
docs.text: "The Wicked" as eschatological antagonist: - MAT 13:38-41 (tares = children of the wicked one; gathered at the end) - MAL 4:1 (day cometh, shall burn as an oven; all the proud, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble) - ISA 14:12, 19 (fall of Lucifer; cast out of grave) - 2 TH 2:8 (that Wicked shall be revealed, consumed by spirit of his mouth) - ISA 11:4 (with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked) — direct source of 2 Thess 2:8's pneuma-of-the-mouth image - DAN 7:25 (wear out saints); DAN 8:23-25 (king of fierce countenance); PSA 58:4 (like the deaf adder)

docs.title: MYSTERY
docs.text:
• 1 CO 2:7 (the wisdom of God in a mystery, hidden)
  • EPH 3:3-6 (mystery made known by revelation — the Gentiles fellow-heirs)
  • EPH 5:32 (great mystery — Christ and the church)
  • COL 1:26-27 (mystery hid from ages — Christ in you, the hope of glory)
  • 1 TI 3:16 (great is the mystery of godliness — God manifest in the flesh)
  • 2 TH 2:7 (the mystery of iniquity doth already work) — direct antithetical pair to 1 TI 3:16
  • REV 10:7 (the mystery of God should be finished)
  • REV 17:5 (upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH)

docs.title: Related Topics Discovered During Gathering
docs.text:
• FALSE CHRIST / FALSE PROPHET (MAT 24:24; MRK 13:22; REV 19:20 chain)
  • IDOLATRY (set up of graven image; cf. DAN 3 Nebuchadnezzar's image and the self-exaltation register)
  • POWERS, PRINCIPALITIES (EPH 6:12 — "wickedness in high places" complements the anomia register)
  • DECEIVE / DECEPTION (MAT 24:4-5, 11, 24 — πλάνη cluster; directly paired with 2 TH 2:9-11 ἐνέργεια πλάνης)
  • JUDGMENT OF GOD (2 TH 2:12 "might be damned" = κριθῶσιν aor-pass-subj; cf. 2 TH 1:5 — "manifest token of the righteous judgment of God")
  • DAY OF THE LORD (1 TH 5:1-3; 2 TH 2:1-2 — the event the man-of-sin's revelation PRECEDES)

docs.title: Topic-Level Cross-Register Observation
docs.text:
The same pericope (2 Thess 2:3-12) is catalogued under FIVE distinct Nave's topics — ANTICHRIST, APOSTASY, BLASPHEMY, TEMPLE, WICKED, and MYSTERY — plus ghost-entries for WORSHIP (via sebasma at v.4) and SANCTUARY (via naos at v.4). This converging-multiplicity is itself an exegetical datum: a single figure is being described whose identification hangs on how these six topical registers are combined.

Research gathered: 2026-04-17 Source: naves_db.py; raw entries at raw-data/naves-entries.txt.

docs.title: Verses — The Man of Sin (2 Thess 2:3-4, 8-12)
docs.text:
All text KJV from D:/bible/tools/data/kjv.txt. Greek parses from greek_parser.py; textual variants from greek_text_compare.py (N1904 vs. TR). Library passage analysis referenced: $GRAMMAR_REF/passages/2thess-2-1-12.md.

Contents: (1) 2 Thess 1-3 (primary passage with full book context) + 1 Thess 4-5 (immediate prior); (2) Pauline naos corpus (deciding literal vs. figurative); (3) Dan 7 little horn; (4) Dan 8 king of fierce countenance; (5) Dan 11:30-45 self-exalting king; (6) Rev 13 parallel beast; (7) Rev 17 beast parallel; (8) Johannine antichristos; (9) Olivet abomination; (10) Isa 14 / Ezek 28 self-deification template + Isa 11:4 destruction template; (11) 1 Tim 4 / 2 Tim 3 / Jude / 2 Pet latter-times apostasy chain; (12) Judas John 17:12 parallel.

docs.title: 2 Thess 1:1-12 (chapter context — the figure's destruction context)
docs.text:
• 1:1-2 Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
  • 1:3-4 We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren … for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:
  • 1:5 [Which is] a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God (ἔνδειγμα τῆς δικαίας κρίσεως τοῦ Θεοῦ), that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also suffer.
  • 1:6 Seeing [it is] a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;
  • 1:7 And to you who are troubled rest with us, when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven (ἐν τῇ ἀποκαλύψει τοῦ Κυρίου Ἰησοῦ) with his mighty angels,
  • 1:8 In flaming fire (ἐν πυρὶ φλογός) taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:
  • 1:9 Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction (ὄλεθρον αἰώνιον) from the presence of the Lord (ἀπὸ προσώπου τοῦ Κυρίου), and from the glory of his power (ἀπὸ τῆς δόξης τῆς ἰσχύος αὐτοῦ);
  • 1:10 When he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired in all them that believe … in that day.
  • 1:11-12 Wherefore also we pray always for you … That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you.

Parsing/annotation (chapter 1, relevant to the figure): - ἀποκαλύψει τοῦ Κυρίου Ἰησοῦ (1:7) is a divinely-structured revelation — the same root as ἀποκαλυφθῇ (2:3), ἀποκαλυφθῆναι (2:6), and ἀποκαλυφθήσεται (2:8). Paul pairs the revelation of Christ against the revelation of the lawless one using the same verb stem. - ὄλεθρον αἰώνιον (1:9) — parallel/companion destruction-word to 2:3's ἀπώλεια and to the consuming verbs ἀνελεῖ and καταργήσει of 2:8. Note: ὄλεθρος (G3639) is distinct from ἀπώλεια (G684) but in NT eschatology they pair (cf. 1 Tim 6:9).

docs.title: 2 Thess 2:1-12 (THE primary passage)
docs.text:
2:1 Now we beseech you (Ἐρωτῶμεν δὲ ὑμᾶς), brethren, by the coming (ὑπὲρ τῆς παρουσίας) of our Lord Jesus Christ, and [by] our gathering together unto him (ἡμῶν ἐπισυναγωγῆς ἐπ' αὐτόν),

2:2 That ye be not soon shaken in mind (εἰς τὸ μὴ ταχέως σαλευθῆναι ὑμᾶς ἀπὸ τοῦ νοός), or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter as from us, as that the day of Christ is at hand (ὡς ὅτι ἐνέστηκεν ἡ ἡμέρα τοῦ Κυρίου — perfect indicative ἐνέστηκεν, "has set in / is present"). - TR variant: ἡ ἡμέρα τοῦ Χριστοῦ ("day of Christ," KJV); N1904 τοῦ Κυρίου. No morphological impact.

2:3 Let no man deceive you (μή τις ὑμᾶς ἐξαπατήσῃ — prohibitive aor. act. subj.) by any means: for [that day shall not come — APODOSIS ELIDED], except there come a falling away first (ὅτι ἐὰν μὴ ἔλθῃ ἡ ἀποστασία πρῶτον), and [except] that man of sin be revealed (καὶ ἀποκαλυφθῇ ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας — aor. pass. subj.), the son of perdition (ὁ υἱὸς τῆς ἀπωλείας);

  • Variant v.3: τῆς ἀνομίας (N1904, ℵ B 0278 33 81 1739 1881 latt) vs. τῆς ἁμαρτίας (A D Ψ 𝔐 KJV/TR). KJV reads "man of sin." Variant changes lexeme (sin → lawlessness) but both genitives are attributive-of-quality. See G458-anomia.md and G266-hamartia.md.
  • Apodosis ellipsis (anacoluthon): the ἐὰν μή clause has no explicit main verb; KJV supplies "[that day shall not come]" implicitly from v.2.
  • Five titles: this verse introduces three of the five titles of the figure — (1) ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας/ἁμαρτίας, (2) ὁ υἱὸς τῆς ἀπωλείας, with v.4 adding (3) the participial title, v.8 adding (4) ὁ ἄνομος, and v.9 indirectly referencing (5) his counter-parousia.
  • πρῶτον is adverbial accusative; it orders the apostasia BEFORE the revelation of the man of sin (and before the unstated "day").

2:4 Who opposeth (ὁ ἀντικείμενος — art. pres. mid./pass. dep. ptcp., G480) and exalteth himself (καὶ ὑπεραιρόμενος — pres. mid./pass. ptcp., G5229; paired middle voice with reflexive-self register) above all (ἐπὶ πάντα) that is called God (λεγόμενον Θεόν) or that is worshipped (ἢ σέβασμα — G4574, the object-of-worship register); so that he as God sitteth (ὥστε αὐτὸν … καθίσαι — result inf., aor. act. inf. κάθίσαι G2523) in the temple of God (εἰς τὸν ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ — G3485 naos = inner sanctuary, not G2411 hieron), shewing himself that he is God (ἀποδεικνύντα ἑαυτὸν ὅτι ἔστιν Θεός — pres. act. ptcp. G584 + reflexive acc. ἑαυτόν + content hoti-clause).

  • TR addition: after ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ TR/Byz inserts ὡς Θεόν ("as God"), which KJV preserves. Critical text omits.
  • apodeiknymi G584 = "display / exhibit / publicly demonstrate." With reflexive + hoti-clause this is a show-he-is-God speech act: a divinity-claim performed by self-display. Only four NT occurrences (Acts 2:22 of Christ's divine accreditation; Acts 25:7; 1 Cor 4:9; here — the counter-mirror).
  • antikeimai + hyperairō paired middles: the only place in the NT where these two middle participles collocate (hyperairō is only 3 NT tokens, all middle). The paired middle voice intensifies the self-focused register (see grammar-ref paired-middle-participles.md).
  • OT source for v.4 epi panta legomenon theon: LXX Theod. Dan 11:36 hypsōthēsetai kai megalynthēsetai epi panta theon. The Greek epi panta theon is verbatim shared. See DOMAIN (5).
  • kathisai aorist infinitive: punctiliar "to seat himself / take his seat" — enthronement/authority-act (the Hebrews-1 "sat down at the right hand" register is the counter-pattern).

2:5 Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you (ἔτι ὢν πρὸς ὑμᾶς), I told you these things (ταῦτα ἔλεγον ὑμῖν — impf. act. — "I was repeatedly telling you")?

  • The ταῦτα ("these things") has referential content Paul's readers knew but which is not expressed in the letter — a floating anaphoric datum that limits what interpreters can reconstruct.

2:6 And now ye know (καὶ νῦν τὸ κατέχον οἴδατε — stative perfect indicative) what withholdeth (τὸ κατέχον — neuter art. pres. act. ptcp., G2722), that he might be revealed (εἰς τὸ ἀποκαλυφθῆναι αὐτὸν — purpose/result articular inf., aor. pass.) in his time (ἐν τῷ αὐτοῦ καιρῷ).

  • Neuter τὸ κατέχον: designates an impersonal/institutional/abstract restraining power ("the thing that restrains"). Same lemma as v.7's masculine form.
  • ἀποκαλυφθῆναι is candidate divine passive (cf. grammar-ref divine-passive.md): God is the unexpressed agent of the revelation, as He is of other apocalyptic unveilings.

2:7 For the mystery of iniquity (τὸ γὰρ μυστήριον ἤδη ἐνεργεῖται τῆς ἀνομίας — "For the mystery of the lawlessness already works") doth already work: only he who now letteth (μόνον ὁ κατέχων ἄρτι — masculine art. pres. act. ptcp., G2722) [will let], until he be taken out of the way (ἕως ἐκ μέσου γένηται — temporal ἕως + aor. mid. subj., "until out of the midst he come to be").

  • Neuter-to-masculine concord shift (τὸ κατέχον → ὁ κατέχων): the gender shift across one verse-boundary is the crux of restrainer identification. Two interpretive mechanisms (from grammar-ref concord-shift-neuter-to-masculine.md):
  • (a) Two different referents (abstract thing in v.6 vs. personal agent in v.7).
  • (b) Same referent viewed from two perspectives — an abstract/institutional aspect and a personal-agent aspect (e.g., Roman state/emperor; Spirit/person; office/officeholder).
  • ἤδη (already): pairs with 1 John 2:18 ἤδη ἀντίχριστοι πολλοὶ γεγόνασιν; mystery-of-iniquity is present-tense in Paul's own day.
  • ἐνεργεῖται (pres. mid. ind.): voice note — middle voice of ἐνεργέω; the mystery is self-energizing / inherently operative, not passive.
  • ἐκ μέσου γένηται: idiomatic "be removed from the midst," not a direct reference to the restrainer's character. Constructio ad sensum: whatever/whoever the restrainer is, he/it is removable.

2:8 And then shall that Wicked be revealed (καὶ τότε ἀποκαλυφθήσεται ὁ ἄνομος — fut. pass. ind., G601; G459 ὁ ἄνομος = substantival adjective "the lawless one"), whom the Lord shall consume (ὃν ὁ Κύριος Ἰησοῦς ἀνελεῖ — fut. act. ind. of G337 ἀναιρέω, "slay/destroy") with the spirit of his mouth (τῷ πνεύματι τοῦ στόματος αὐτοῦ — dative of means/instrument; ISA 11:4 LXX allusion), and shall destroy (καὶ καταργήσει — fut. act. ind., G2673 "abolish/render inoperative") with the brightness of his coming (τῇ ἐπιφανείᾳ τῆς παρουσίας αὐτοῦ — dative of means, G2015 ἐπιφάνεια + G3952 παρουσία; this doublet occurs only here in Paul).

  • Three future indicatives cluster at v.8 — all other finite forms in the pericope are present, imperfect, aorist subjunctive, or perfect. The future tenses are reserved for the climactic revelation-and-destruction panel.
  • Two destruction instruments balance chiastically: pneumati stomatos (Isa 11:4 echo) || epiphaneia parousias (Christological climax; epiphaneia is the visible-manifestation noun; parousia is the advent-noun). Both are datives of means.
  • ἀνελεῖ vs. TR ἀναλώσει (G355 "consume, use up"): N1904 and Byzantine majority read ἀνελεῖ; A D F G 1739 latt read ἀναλώσει. Both are fut. act. ind.; lexical variant only.
  • TR variant also omits Ἰησοῦς after ὁ Κύριος — KJV includes.

2:9 [Even him], whose coming (οὗ ἐστιν ἡ παρουσία — deliberate lexical collision: same noun παρουσία as v.1 and v.8 of Christ's) is after the working of Satan (κατ' ἐνέργειαν τοῦ Σατανᾶ — subjective genitive; Satan is the working source, G1753 energeia + G4567) with all power and signs and lying wonders (ἐν πάσῃ δυνάμει καὶ σημείοις καὶ τέρασιν ψεύδους — triple instrumental; ψεύδους modifies τέρασιν or all three — Greek ambiguity),

  • The counter-parousia. Paul uses the same word for Christ's coming (v.1, v.8) and for the lawless one's coming (v.9). This is deliberate typological mirroring (the figure is the counterfeit of Christ).
  • energeia tou Satana: subjective-genitive pattern established in grammar-ref energeia-subjective-genitive.md. Same grammatical frame as Eph 1:19 (ἐνέργειαν τοῦ κράτους τῆς ἰσχύος αὐτοῦ) — but here the agent is Satan.
  • σημείοις καὶ τέρασιν: standard NT "signs and wonders" doublet (G4592 + G5059). Paul inverts — here these are false signs and wonders, with ψεύδους (of falsehood) as attributive genitive. Paired with Rev 13:13-14 σημεῖα μεγάλα of the second beast / false prophet.

2:10 And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness (καὶ ἐν πάσῃ ἀπάτῃ ἀδικίας — attributive "unrighteousness-deceit") in them that perish (τοῖς ἀπολλυμένοις — pres. mid./pass. ptcp., G622 cognate to G684 apōleia — "those in the process of perishing"); because they received not the love of the truth (ἀνθ' ὧν τὴν ἀγάπην τῆς ἀληθείας οὐκ ἐδέξαντο — aor. mid. ind. with ground-clause idiom ἀνθ' ὧν "because, in return for which"), that they might be saved (εἰς τὸ σωθῆναι αὐτούς).

  • τοῖς ἀπολλυμένοις (perishing) is the morphological cognate of ἀπώλεια (perdition v.3). The son of perdition draws into his apoleia those likewise labeled with the same root.

2:11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion (καὶ διὰ τοῦτο πέμπει αὐτοῖς ὁ Θεὸς ἐνέργειαν πλάνης — lit. "God sends them a working of delusion"; same noun ἐνέργεια as v.9 but with θεός as subject and πλάνη G4106 as attributive), that they should believe a lie (εἰς τὸ πιστεῦσαι αὐτοὺς τῷ ψεύδει):

  • Three ἐνέργεια subjects in vv.7, 9, 11: the mystery of anomia (v.7 ἐνεργεῖται), Satan (v.9 τοῦ Σατανᾶ), God (v.11 ὁ Θεὸς πέμπει ἐνέργειαν πλάνης). The same verb-family ranges across three agent-types — the divine judicial release of deception follows the rejection of truth.

2:12 That they all might be damned (ἵνα κριθῶσιν πάντες — aor. pass. subj. G2919 "be judged") who believed not the truth (οἱ μὴ πιστεύσαντες τῇ ἀληθείᾳ — aor. act. ptcp. negated), but had pleasure in unrighteousness (ἀλλὰ εὐδοκήσαντες τῇ ἀδικίᾳ).

  • The passive subjunctive κριθῶσιν closes the pericope with God as implicit judging agent.

docs.title: 2 Thess 2:13-17 (immediate context — the antithesis)
docs.text:
• 2:13 But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation (εἵλατο ὑμᾶς ὁ Θεὸς ἀπαρχὴν εἰς σωτηρίαν — TR variant ἀπ' ἀρχῆς) through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth (ἐν ἁγιασμῷ πνεύματος καὶ πίστει ἀληθείας):
  • 2:14 Whereunto he called you by our gospel, to the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
  • 2:15 Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions (κρατεῖτε τὰς παραδόσεις — G2902 "hold fast" — notably NOT G2722 katechō) which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.
  • 2:16-17 Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, … Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work.

Note: The antithesis to the lawlessness-pattern is παράδοσις (tradition/teaching) held fast by the saints. The figure's operation works against truth; the saints' work is holding fast the truth.

docs.title: 2 Thess 3:1-18 (closing context)
docs.text:
• 3:1-5 Final exhortations: the word of the Lord have free course; Lord keep from evil; direct hearts into love of God.
  • 3:6-15 Withdraw from disorderly brothers — ataktōs (ἀτάκτως) behavior marked for discipline; work-ethic commands.
  • 3:16-18 Peace benediction; Paul's own handwriting as authentication mark against forged letters (ἐπιστολή echoed from 2:2).

docs.title: 1 Thess 4:13-5:11 (the parousia teaching that 2 Thess 2 corrects)
docs.text:
• 4:13-14 But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep … them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.
  • 4:15 For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive [and] remain unto the coming of the Lord (εἰς τὴν παρουσίαν τοῦ Κυρίου) shall not prevent them which are asleep.
  • 4:16 For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
  • 4:17 Then we which are alive [and] remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air (εἰς ἀπάντησιν τοῦ Κυρίου εἰς ἀέρα): and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
  • 5:1-2 But of the times and the seasons (χρόνων καὶ τῶν καιρῶν), brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you. For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night (ἡμέρα Κυρίου ὡς κλέπτης ἐν νυκτί).
  • 5:3 For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction (αἰφνίδιος … ὄλεθρος) cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape.
  • 5:4-9 Sons-of-light vs. sons-of-darkness discourse; God has not appointed us to wrath (εἰς ὀργήν) but to obtain salvation.

Inter-letter context: The Thessalonian confusion 2 Thess 2:2 corrects ("day of the Lord hath arrived") is the same eschatological horizon 1 Thess 4-5 taught — the letter is a corrective against rushed-realized-eschatology. The παρουσία / ἡμέρα Κυρίου pair spans both letters.

docs.title: DOMAIN (2) — Pauline naos corpus (decides literal vs. figurative)
1 Cor 3:16-17 — believers as naos
docs.text:
• 3:16 Know ye not that ye are the temple of God (ναὸς Θεοῦ ἐστε), and that the Spirit of God dwelleth in you (τὸ Πνεῦμα τοῦ Θεοῦ οἰκεῖ ἐν ὑμῖν)?
  • 3:17 If any man defile the temple of God (τὸν ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ), him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy (ὁ ναὸς τοῦ Θεοῦ ἅγιός ἐστιν), which [temple] ye are.

Parsing note: ναὸς Θεοῦ ἐστε — predicate-nominative construction: "a temple-of-God you are" (collective/corporate). G3485 naos (inner sanctuary), not G2411 hieron. The exact construct ὁ ναὸς τοῦ Θεοῦ appears here — identical surface form as 2 Thess 2:4.

docs.title: 1 Cor 6:19 — body as naos
docs.text:
• 6:19 What? know ye not that your body is the temple (ναός) of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own?

Parse: τὸ σῶμα ὑμῶν ναὸς τοῦ ἐν ὑμῖν Ἁγίου Πνεύματός ἐστιν. Individual body = naos. Indwelling agent is the Holy Spirit.

docs.title: 2 Cor 6:16 — ye are the naos of the living God
docs.text:
• 6:16 And what agreement hath the temple of God with idols? (τίς δὲ συγκατάθεσις ναῷ Θεοῦ μετὰ εἰδώλων;) for ye are the temple of the living God (ἡμεῖς γὰρ ναὸς Θεοῦ ἐσμεν ζῶντος); as God hath said, I will dwell in them (ἐνοικήσω ἐν αὐτοῖς), and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

Parse: The ναός / εἴδωλα antithesis is exactly the counter to 2 Thess 2:4's man of sin who sits in God's naos yet displays himself as divine. Paul cites Lev 26:11-12 / Ezek 37:27 — God's indwelling in His people IS the temple.

docs.title: Eph 2:19-22 — building growing into a holy naos
docs.text:
• 2:19 Now therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints, and of the household of God (οἰκεῖοι τοῦ Θεοῦ);
  • 2:20 And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone (ἀκρογωνιαίου);
  • 2:21 In whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord (πᾶσα οἰκοδομὴ συναρμολογουμένη αὔξει εἰς ναὸν ἅγιον ἐν Κυρίῳ);
  • 2:22 In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit (εἰς κατοικητήριον τοῦ Θεοῦ ἐν Πνεύματι).

Parse: ναὸν ἅγιον ἐν Κυρίῳ — this is the growing organic structure of the believing community, Christ as cornerstone, apostles/prophets as foundation. κατοικητήριον τοῦ Θεοῦ reinforces the in-dwelling register. This is THE primary text Bohr/Newton/Clarke use to route "temple of God = church" in 2 Thess 2:4.

docs.title: 1 Tim 3:15 — house of God (not naos but complementary)
docs.text:
• 3:15 But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God (ἐν οἴκῳ Θεοῦ), which is the church of the living God (ἥτις ἐστὶν ἐκκλησία Θεοῦ ζῶντος), the pillar and ground of the truth (στῦλος καὶ ἑδραίωμα τῆς ἀληθείας).

Parse: οἶκος not ναός here, but the principle is identical — the ekklēsia is the locus of God's dwelling for the Pauline corpus.

docs.title: 1 Pet 2:4-10 — living stones / spiritual house
docs.text:
• 2:4 To whom coming, [as unto] a living stone (λίθον ζῶντα), disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, [and] precious,
  • 2:5 Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house (οἶκος πνευματικός), an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.
  • 2:6 … Behold, I lay in Sion a chief corner stone (ἀκρογωνιαῖον), elect, precious …
  • 2:9 But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people.

Parse: 1 Peter uses οἶκος πνευματικός (spiritual house), not naos, but the building-metaphor cluster (lithos, akrogōniaios, oikodomē) is the same as Eph 2:19-22. Together these establish the NT pattern: believers are the living, in-built sanctuary.

docs.title: Rev 11:1-2 — literal vs. symbolic naos
docs.text:
• 11:1 And there was given me a reed like unto a rod: and the angel stood, saying, Rise, and measure the temple of God (ἔγειρε καὶ μέτρησον τὸν ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ), and the altar, and them that worship therein.
  • 11:2 But the court which is without the temple (τὴν αὐλὴν τὴν ἔξωθεν τοῦ ναοῦ) leave out, and measure it not; for it is given unto the Gentiles: and the holy city shall they tread under foot forty [and] two months.

Parse: Revelation 11 apocalyptically splits ναός (measured; associated with worshippers) from αὐλή (court; given to Gentiles). This is measurement-by-symbolic-boundary, not literal Herodian temple data. The 42 months (= 1260 days = time-times-half) matches Dan 7:25.

docs.title: John 2:19-21 — Christ's body as naos
docs.text:
• 2:19 Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up (λύσατε τὸν ναὸν τοῦτον).
  • 2:20 Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this temple (ὁ ναὸς οὗτος) in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?
  • 2:21 But he spake of the temple of his body (περὶ τοῦ ναοῦ τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ).

Parse: Johannine witness: naos can refer to Christ's body. The earliest Christian use of ναός applies it metaphorically to a person.

docs.title: Rev 21:22 — no naos in the eschatological city
docs.text: • 21:22 And I saw no temple therein (ναὸν οὐκ εἶδον ἐν αὐτῇ): for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it (ὁ γὰρ Κύριος ὁ Θεὸς ὁ Παντοκράτωρ ναὸς αὐτῆς ἐστιν καὶ τὸ Ἀρνίον).

docs.title: DOMAIN (3) — Daniel 7 (little horn)
docs.text:
• 7:1-6 Four beasts from the sea (lion, bear, leopard, terrible fourth beast).
  • 7:7 After this I saw … a fourth beast, dreadful and terrible, and strong exceedingly; and it had great iron teeth: it devoured and brake in pieces, and stamped the residue with the feet of it: and it [was] diverse from all the beasts that [were] before it; and it had ten horns.
  • 7:8 I considered the horns, and, behold, there came up among them another little horn (qeren ze'eyrah), before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots: and, behold, in this horn [were] eyes like the eyes of man, and a mouth speaking great things (u-pum memalal rabrebāan).
  • 7:9-10 I beheld till the thrones were cast down, and the Ancient of days did sit … the judgment was set, and the books were opened.
  • 7:11 I beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spake: I beheld [even] till the beast was slain, and his body destroyed, and given to the burning flame.
  • 7:12 As concerning the rest of the beasts, they had their dominion taken away: yet their lives were prolonged for a season and time.
  • 7:13-14 Son of Man comes with the clouds, receives dominion, glory, kingdom.
  • 7:17 These great beasts, which are four, [are] four kings — malkhīn (Aramaic "kings"). v.23 interprets this as "kingdoms" (malkhū). Note this is the single explicit scriptural precedent for king/kingdom equivalence — relevant for whether ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας + singular must denote an individual person.
  • 7:20 And of the ten horns that [were] in his head, and [of] the other which came up, and before whom three fell; even [of] that horn that had eyes, and a mouth that spake very great things, whose look [was] more stout than his fellows.
  • 7:21 I beheld, and the same horn made war with the saints, and prevailed against them;
  • 7:22 Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment was given to the saints of the most High; and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom.
  • 7:23 Thus he said, The fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth.
  • 7:24 And the ten horns out of this kingdom [are] ten kings [that] shall arise: and another shall rise after them; and he shall be diverse from the first, and he shall subdue three kings.
  • 7:25 And he shall speak [great] words against the most High (u-milīn l-tsad 'illā'āh y-mallīl), and shall wear out the saints of the most High (u-l-qaddīshē 'elyōnīn y-ballē), and think to change times and laws (v-yis-bar l-hashnāyāh zimnīn v-dāt): and they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and the dividing of time ('ad 'iddān v-'iddānīn u-plag 'iddān).
  • 7:26 But the judgment shall sit, and they shall take away his dominion, to consume and to destroy [it] unto the end.
  • 7:27 And the kingdom … shall be given to the people of the saints of the most High.

Parsing/annotation: - u-pum memalal rabrebāan ("and a mouth speaking great things") — LXX Theod. στόμα λαλοῦν μεγάλα. This exact phrase is verbatim quoted in Rev 13:5 (στόμα λαλοῦν μεγάλα). See grammar-ref rev-13-5.md. - 'iddān v-'iddānīn u-plag 'iddān ("time and times and dividing of a time") — establishes the 3.5 time-units figure later matched by Rev 12:14 (καιρὸν καὶ καιροὺς καὶ ἥμισυ καιροῦ), Rev 11:2-3 (42 months / 1260 days), and Rev 13:5 (42 months). - y-ballē (Aramaic pael "to wear out, exhaust") — the idea is attrition, not outright destruction. Saints delivered into his hand for 3.5 times. - y-hashnāyāh zimnīn v-dāt ("change times and law"): programmatic opposition to divine temporal/legal order — conceptually paralleled by Paul's ἀνομία (anti-law) register. See grammar-ref dan-7-25-26.md.

2 Thess 2 overlap markers: - Self-exaltation / great words against God (Dan 7:8, 11, 20, 25 ↔ 2 Thess 2:4). - War against saints (Dan 7:21 ↔ 2 Thess 2 destruction of saints implicit in the pericope's opposition register). - Change of times-and-laws (Dan 7:25 ↔ anomia-institutionalized, 2 Thess 2:3, 7, 8). - Judgment-based destruction (Dan 7:26 "take away his dominion, to consume" ↔ 2 Thess 2:8 ἀνελεῖ + καταργήσει). - Time-scope: "time, times, and dividing of time" (Dan 7:25) — 3.5 periods; this is the primary OT time-scope text for a multi-century rather than momentary figure.

docs.title: DOMAIN (4) — Daniel 8 (king of fierce countenance)
docs.text:
• 8:1-8 Ram with two horns (Media-Persia); he-goat (Greece) with notable horn (first king), great horn broken, four notable horns in its place.
  • 8:9 And out of one of them came forth a little horn, which waxed exceeding great, toward the south, and toward the east, and toward the pleasant [land] (ha-tsevī).
  • 8:10 And it waxed great, [even] to the host of heaven; and it cast down [some] of the host and of the stars to the ground, and stamped upon them.
  • 8:11 Yea, he magnified [himself] even to the prince of the host (v-'ad sar ha-tsavā' higdīl), and by him the daily [sacrifice] was taken away (ha-tāmīd), and the place of his sanctuary was cast down (hushlakh m-khōn miqdāshō).
  • 8:12 And an host was given [him] against the daily [sacrifice] by reason of transgression (b-pesha'), and it cast down the truth ('emet) to the ground; and it practised, and prospered.
  • 8:13 … How long [shall be] the vision [concerning] the daily [sacrifice], and the transgression of desolation (ha-pesha' shōmēm), to give both the sanctuary (qōdesh) and the host to be trodden under foot?
  • 8:14 And he said unto me, Unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed (v-nitsdaq qōdesh — "and the sanctuary shall be justified/vindicated").
  • 8:15-22 Gabriel's interpretation (ram = Media-Persia; rough goat = Greece; first king = Alexander; four kingdoms).
  • 8:23 And in the latter time of their kingdom, when the transgressors are come to the full (k-hatēm ha-pōsh'īm), a king of fierce countenance (melekh 'az pānīm), and understanding dark sentences, shall stand up.
  • 8:24 And his power shall be mighty, but not by his own power (v-lō' v-khōchō): and he shall destroy wonderfully, and shall prosper, and practise, and shall destroy the mighty and the holy people (v-hishchīt 'atsūmīm v-'am q-dōshīm).
  • 8:25 And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify [himself] in his heart (u-bi-l-vāvō yagdīl), and by peace shall destroy many: he shall also stand up against the Prince of princes (v-'al sar sārīm ya'amōd); but he shall be broken without hand (u-b-'efes yad yishāvēr).

Parsing/annotation: - higdīl (Hiphil) / yagdīl (Qal) — the verb גדל (to become great, magnify) — semantic link to LXX μεγαλύνω which is the same root Paul echoes in ὑπεραιρόμενος (though Paul uses compound hyper-airō). See grammar-ref dan-8-23-25.md. - sar sārīm "prince of princes" — superlative construct; the rebellion is against the highest princely authority. - b-'efes yad yishāvēr "without hand he shall be broken" — Niphal passive, agent-denying idiom. Destruction is by divine agency, not human. Direct conceptual match to 2 Thess 2:8 τῇ ἐπιφανείᾳ τῆς παρουσίας αὐτοῦ (destruction by Christ's advent, not human armies). - ha-tāmīd (the continual/daily) — taken away; sanctuary cast down.

2 Thess 2 overlap markers: - Self-magnification (Dan 8:11, 25 ↔ 2 Thess 2:4). - Not by own power / yet mighty (Dan 8:24 ↔ 2 Thess 2:9 "according to energeia of Satan"). - Destruction of saints/holy-people (Dan 8:24 ↔ implicit in 2 Thess 2 by reading-chain). - Broken without hand (Dan 8:25 ↔ 2 Thess 2:8 destruction by parousia/epiphaneia, not by human force).

docs.title: DOMAIN (5) — Daniel 11:30-45 (self-exalting king)
docs.text:
• 11:30 For the ships of Chittim shall come against him: therefore he shall be grieved, and return, and have indignation against the holy covenant (berīt qōdesh): so shall he do; he shall even return, and have intelligence with them that forsake the holy covenant.
  • 11:31 And arms shall stand on his part, and they shall pollute the sanctuary of strength (v-chil-l-lū ha-miqdāsh ha-mā'ōz), and shall take away the daily (v-hēsīrū ha-tāmīd), and they shall place the abomination that maketh desolate (v-nāt-nū ha-shiqqūts m-shōmēm).
