docs.title: Focus Areas
docs.text:
1. Five-view steelman from biblical data only. The scoping register has catalogued five competing interpretations. WHY: the prior study concluded historicist without engaging the others on their own exegetical merits; the gaps list (items 1, 4, 5) require each view to be reconstructed from biblical evidence alone before comparison. HOW: For each of the five views (Historicist; Preterist — Nero/Jewish-priesthood/Antiochus-type; Dispensational Futurist; Idealist; Roman-emperor/Caligula-type), produce a point-by-point fit against ALL eight textual markers in 2 Thess 2:3-12 (apostasia precondition; anthropos-class; self-exaltation; naos-location; kathisai-authority-act; to/ho-katechon restrainer; mystery-of-iniquity-already-working v.7; consumed-by-parousia destruction). Flag which markers each view strains. Do NOT pre-favor the historicist view.

  2. Lexical register of "man of sin/lawlessness" — anthropos-tēs-X construction. WHY: gap-2 and gap-8 both turn on whether anthropos + definite article + genitive forces a single human OR admits a class/succession (Dan 7:17 LXX precedent). Textual variant hamartias vs. anomias compounds this. HOW: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G5207-huios.md for the parallel "son of X" pattern; then read generated G444-anthropos.md, G266-hamartia.md, G458-anomia.md, G684-apoleia.md. Run greek_text_compare.py on 2 Thess 2:3 for the variant. Run greek_parser.py --lemma on anomia (G458) and anomos (G459) to list all NT occurrences. Compare Semitic "son of X" idiom (cf. $WORD_STUDIES/TR-ben-huios.md) against the Greek anthropos-tes-X. Check Dan 7:17 LXX for the "four men"="four kingdoms" precedent.

  3. The apostasia precondition — the falling away must come first. WHY: v.3 makes the apostasia temporally PRIOR to the revelation of the man of sin; definite article forces "THE apostasia" as a specific prophesied event (Acts 21:21; 1 Tim 4:1 content). HOW: READ generated G646-apostasia.md and G868-aphistēmi.md. Retrieve LXX occurrences (Josh 22:22; 2 Chr 29:19; 33:19; Jer 2:19). Parse Acts 21:21 and 1 Tim 4:1-3 with greek_parser.py. Retrieve ACT 20:29-30 (wolves arising from within); 2 PE 2:1-3; Jude 1:4. Ask: is the apostasia (a) the AD 70 Jewish apostasy, (b) the post-apostolic Constantinian-era apostasy, (c) a still-future apostasy, or (d) the perennial anomia principle?

  4. Naos tou theou — literal vs. figurative, decided by exegesis, not assumption. WHY: gap-4 — the prior study stated figurative without steelmanning literal. 51 AD dating keeps literal open. HOW: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G3485-naos.md and G2411-hieron.md (already present) to establish the semantic distinction. Retrieve and parse: 1 CO 3:16-17, 2 CO 6:16, EPH 2:19-22 (four Pauline data points); 1 PE 2:4-5; HEB 3:6; HEB 10:21 (figurative support); MAT 24:1-2, 24:15; JHN 2:19-21; REV 11:1-2 (literal data); ACT 7:47-50 ("God dwelleth not in houses made with hands"). Run concept_context.py "2TH 2:4" --scope author for Paul's own naos usage pattern.

  5. The restrainer — neuter to katechon + masculine ho katechōn. WHY: gap-5 — the gender shift is the crux. Catalogue all 5 candidates biblically. HOW: READ generated G2722-katechō.md and the generated "Greek neuter-masculine concord shift" grammar entry. Parse vv.6-7 with greek_parser.py. Catalogue candidates: (a) pagan Roman state (neuter) / emperor (masc); (b) Holy Spirit / church preaching (spiritual/personal); (c) divine decree / angel Michael (Dan 10:13, 12:1); (d) Jewish/Jerusalem authority / James; (e) gospel-era proclamation / ton euangelion. Evaluate each against: (i) active in Paul's day? (ii) removable "out of the way"? (iii) dual neuter/masculine fit? (iv) named or only inferable?

  6. Mystery of lawlessness "already working" (v.7) — temporal scope. WHY: forces the reading to begin in Paul's day AND climax at the parousia. An individual-only reading in the 1st century fails; an exclusively-future reading ignores the "already." HOW: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G3466-mysterion.md and the generated G1754-energeō.md. Retrieve 1 JN 2:18 ("even now are there many antichrists"); 1 JN 4:3 ("even now already is it in the world"); ACT 20:29-30 (Paul's Miletus warning — wolves ARISING, future tense); 2 PE 2:1 ("there were false prophets… even as there shall be false teachers"); 2 TI 3:1-9 (perilous times SHALL come, but the teachers of v.5 are to be turned away from NOW). Map the already/not-yet dialectic.

  7. OT template mapping — Dan 7:25, 8:23-25, 11:36-37, Isa 14:13-14, Ezek 28:2. WHY: gap-3 and gap-7 — the Greek of 2 Thess 2:4 has not been formally compared to the LXX/Theodotion Greek of each candidate OT source. HOW: READ $GRAMMAR_REF/passages/dan-7-25-26.md. Generate passage analyses for Dan 11:36-37, Dan 8:23-25, Isa 14:12-15 + Ezek 28:2-9. Parse each with hebrew_parser.py. Compare vocabulary chains: hypsōthēsetai / megalynthēsetai / hyperairomenos (self-exaltation); theon / panta theon / epi panta legomenon theon (god-comparison); kathisai / yashab / katoikian theou (sit-in-divine-seat); 42-months / time-times-half / 1260-days (time horizon). Produce an alignment table.

  8. Rev 13:5-8 + Rev 17:3-6 + Rev 19:19-20 as parallel NT developments. WHY: SP115 (revs-46) documents a seven-point >75% element-by-element correspondence. SP117 flags the ONE-entity-in-Paul maps to TWO-entities-in-Rev-13 complication. HOW: READ $GRAMMAR_REF/passages/rev-13-1-10.md, rev-13-3.md, rev-13-5.md. Retrieve and parse REV 13:5-8 (42 months, blasphemy, saints war, all-worship); REV 17:3-6 (scarlet beast, MYSTERION BABYLON, blasphemy names, drunken with saints' blood); REV 17:8,11 ("goeth into apoleia" — matches huios tēs apōleias); REV 19:19-20 (destruction parallel to 2 Thess 2:8). Evaluate SP117: does Paul collapse Rev's two beasts into one figure, or does Paul's "man of sin" correspond to one specifically?

  9. Johannine antichristos — the vocabulary bridge Paul does NOT use. WHY: gap-6 — Paul never uses antichristos (G500); John never uses anomos or huios apōleias. The common identification is INFERRED. HOW: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G500-antichristos.md. Parse 1 JN 2:18-23; 1 JN 4:1-6; 2 JN 7-11 with greek_parser.py. List every John-term: antichristos (plural AND singular); pneuma tēs planēs; pneuma tou antichristou; "even now already in the world." Compare against Paul's inventory: anthropos tēs anomias / hamartias; huios tēs apōleias; ho anomos; to mysterion tēs anomias; energeia tou Satana. Ask: are John's "many antichrists" (plural, "even now") the same phenomenon as Paul's mysterion anomias already-working (also "even now"), with the final antichristos singular (1 Jn 2:18 "ho antichristos erchetai") = Paul's singular ho anomos to be revealed? If yes, lexical bridge stands; if no, the identification falls.

  10. Destruction at the parousia — single moment vs. progressive. WHY: v.8 uses two verbs (anelei + katargēsei) and two instruments (pneumati stomatos + epiphaneia parousias). Does this mean (a) a single climactic event, (b) two sequential events (consuming by word-proclamation during history; destruction at parousia), or (c) a doublet for rhetorical emphasis? This decision discriminates between historicist-progressive and strictly-futurist readings. HOW: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G601-apokalypto.md (the timing-logic word), G2673-katargeō.md, G2015-epiphaneia.md, G3952-parousia.md; and generated G337-anaireō.md. Parse v.8 with greek_parser.py. Trace Isa 11:4 LXX (direct source of pneumati tou stomatos). Check the katargeo-by-epiphaneia pattern elsewhere in Paul (1 Cor 15:24-26; 2 Tim 1:10). Check Dan 8:25 "broken without hand" and Dan 11:45 "he shall come to his end" for OT destruction-pattern parallel.

docs.title: External Corpus Leads (from 00-references.md — as research directives)
docs.text:
1. Bohr/Froom/Newton/Clarke consensus: temple-of-God = Christian church via Eph 2:19-22 + 1 Pet 2:4-5. Verify: Parse EPH 2:19-22 and 1 PE 2:4-5 with greek_parser.py. Confirm every Pauline naos-tou-theou usage (1 Cor 3:16-17; 2 Cor 6:16; Eph 2:21) forms a consistent figurative pattern. But ALSO steelman literal: 2 Thess is 51 AD, Herodian temple standing; Matt 24:15 is within prophetic horizon; Caligula 40 AD crisis in living memory. Produce a decision trade-off table.

  2. Bohr's two-phase papal rule with deadly-wound interval. Verify: does 2 Thess 2 alone support a two-phase reading? The text gives: mysterion already working (v.7) → taken-out-of-way → revealed (v.3,6,8) → destroyed at parousia (v.8). Evaluate whether a deadly-wound interval fits between "revealed" and "destroyed," OR whether it's a Rev 13:3 concept imported into 2 Thess 2. The text itself may not require it.

  3. Matthew Henry's chain: Dan 11:36-37 → 1 Tim 4:1 → papal celibacy and Mahuzzim-saints. Verify: (a) parse DAN 11:36-37 Hebrew with hebrew_parser.py — "desire of women" (chemdat nashim) semantic range? (b) parse 1 TI 4:1-3 Greek — kōlyontōn gamein (forbidding to marry) lexical overlap with Dan 11:37? (c) trace Mahuzzim (H4581 ma'oz) in DAN 11:38-39 — "god of forces" vs. "god of fortresses"; connect or disconnect from saint-veneration.

  4. Guinness's "commanded sin" argument for unique papal identification. Verify: does anomia (G458) connote organized/institutionalized opposition to law rather than mere personal wickedness? Check the anomia vs. hamartia semantic distinction; check 1 Jn 3:4 "sin IS anomia" for equation; check Dan 7:25 "think to change TIMES and LAWS" as evidence of institutional law-opposition (not personal transgression).

  5. Newton/Clarke "pope in general, not any one pope" succession reading. Verify: Greek ho anthropos tēs anomias + definite article + singular. Does this force a single historical person (futurist), or does the anthropos-as-class-representative idiom permit succession-as-one (historicist), or does the substantive-adjectival anomos (v.8) allow a personified principle (idealist)? Run the generated Greek-anthropos-individual-vs-type grammar entry against Dan 7:17 LXX ("these great beasts, which are FOUR, are four kings" — four persons or four empires?).

  6. EGW's "Mystery of Iniquity" as chapter title in EW/SH/SR/1SG. Verify only by Scripture: the content EGW builds (pagan-Roman-Constantinian-papal progression) is historical-evidence commentary, not biblical exegesis. Do not weight as independent evidence. Note in Research Instructions to treat as lead only.

  7. LXX Dan 7:8 stoma laloun megala = Rev 13:5 verbatim. Extend: run the same verbatim tracer on 2 Thess 2:4 vs. LXX Dan 11:36 (hypsōthēsetai… megalynthēsetai epi panta theon) vs. Isa 14:13-14 LXX (anabēsomai eis ton ouranon… kathiō en orei hypsēlō) vs. Ezek 28:2 LXX. Tabulate lexical overlaps.

  8. Catholic counter-reading: Antichrist as future individual, not the papal office. Verify: this is the same position as the dispensational-futurist steelman. Treat as view #3, not as independent claim.

docs.title: Research Instructions
docs.text:
You are the Research Agent. Execute this study by:

  1. Read the SKILL.md at C:/Users/Michael/.claude/skills/bible-study5/SKILL.md for full tool documentation and principles.
  2. Read your agent instructions at C:/Users/Michael/.claude/skills/bible-study5/agents/research-agent.md.
  3. Read ALL library entries listed in "Available Library Entries" above — incorporate their data into research files. Do NOT re-derive.
  4. Follow the greek-grammar-analysis workflow with heavy answer-question synthesis component — this is a who/what-is-it question that turns on Greek grammar + OT templates + NT parallel-passage integration.
  5. Write research files to this folder:
  6. 01-topics.md — Nave's topics retrieved in full: ANTICHRIST, APOSTASY, BLASPHEMY, TEMPLE, SANCTUARY, WORSHIP, WICKED (PEOPLE), PRIEST (PAPACY likely absent — note). Organize entries by subtopic.
  7. 02-verses.md — All verse texts retrieved with full CHAPTER context for primary verses.
  8. 04-word-studies.md — Strong's research:
    • FROM LIBRARY (read, don't re-derive): G500, G601, G3466, G3485, G2411, G2673, G5207, G2316, G2476, G3952, G2015, G1411, G225, G2362, G1785, G1849, G1577, G1404, G2342, G1228, G2643, G2647, G161, G1325 plus TR-chattaah-hamartia, TR-avon-adikia, TR-ben-huios, TR-chereb-rhomphaia
    • FRESH LOOKUPS (read newly-generated entries): G646, G444, G266, G458, G459, G684, G480, G5229, G4574, G2523, G584, G2722, G1753, G1754, G337, G4106, G4105, G868, G4592, G5059
  9. raw-data/ — Raw tool output organized by category (grammar-parsing/, parallels/, concept-context/, variants/, nave/)
  10. Do NOT write 03-analysis.md or CONCLUSION.md — those are for the analysis agent.

docs.title: Specific Research Directives
docs.text:
1. Priority verses to retrieve with FULL CHAPTER context:
  2. 2 Thess 1:1-2:17 (whole pericope including the signature chapter-1 "flaming fire taking vengeance" context)
  3. 1 Thess 4:13-5:11 (the earlier parousia teaching this letter is correcting)
  4. Daniel 7 (whole chapter — beasts, little horn, judgment)
  5. Daniel 8 (whole chapter — ram/goat, little horn, 2300 evenings-mornings)
  6. Daniel 11 (whole chapter — historical march to the self-exalting king)
  7. Revelation 13 (whole chapter — both beasts)
  8. Revelation 17 (whole chapter — harlot and scarlet beast, MYSTERION BABYLON)
  9. 1 John 2 (whole chapter — many antichrists)
  10. Isaiah 14 (whole chapter — fall of Lucifer/king of Babylon)
  11. Ezekiel 28 (whole chapter — prince of Tyre + king of Tyre)
  12. Matt 24 and Mark 13 (Olivet discourse)

  13. 1 Timothy 4 and 2 Timothy 3 (latter-times apostasy teaching)

  14. Required cross-testament parallels (run BOTH --hybrid-ot AND --hybrid-nt):

  15. 2 TH 2:3
  16. 2 TH 2:4
  17. 2 TH 2:7
  18. 2 TH 2:8
  19. 2 TH 2:9
  20. DAN 11:36
  21. DAN 7:25
  22. ISA 14:13
  23. EZK 28:2
  24. REV 13:5
  25. REV 17:8

  26. 1 JN 2:18

  27. Required Greek/Hebrew parsing:

  28. greek_parser.py --verse for EACH of 2 TH 2:1 through 2:12 (every verse)
  29. greek_parser.py --verse REV 13:5-8
  30. greek_parser.py --verse REV 17:3-6
  31. greek_parser.py --verse REV 19:19-20
  32. greek_parser.py --verse 1 JN 2:18; 1 JN 2:22; 1 JN 4:3; 2 JN 1:7
  33. greek_parser.py --verse MAT 24:15; MRK 13:14
  34. greek_parser.py --verse 1 TI 4:1-3; 2 TI 3:1-5
  35. greek_parser.py --verse 1 CO 3:16-17; 2 CO 6:16; EPH 2:19-22; 1 PE 2:4-10
  36. hebrew_parser.py --verse DAN 7:24-26; DAN 8:9-14; DAN 8:23-25; DAN 11:30-38; DAN 11:45; DAN 12:11
  37. hebrew_parser.py --verse ISA 14:12-15; EZK 28:2-9; ISA 11:4
  38. greek_text_compare.py --verse 2 TH 2:3 (hamartias vs. anomias variant)
  39. greek_text_compare.py --verse 2 TH 2:7 (ho katechōn variants)
  40. greek_text_compare.py --verse 2 TH 2:8 (anelei vs. analōsei variant)
  41. greek_parser.py --lemma ἀνομία (G458) — list all 15 NT occurrences

  42. greek_parser.py --lemma ἄνομος (G459) — list all 10 NT occurrences

  43. Required concept_context passes:

    • concept_context.py "2TH 2:3"
    • concept_context.py "2TH 2:4"
    • concept_context.py "2TH 2:7"
    • concept_context.py "2TH 2:8"
    • concept_context.py "DAN 11:36"
    • concept_context.py "2TH 2:4" --scope author (Paul's own naos/sebasma register)

  44. Required word traces (search_strongs.py --verses):

    • G646 apostasia (appears only 2x NT — 2 Thess 2:3, Acts 21:21)
    • G684 apoleia (18x — map the Jn 17:12 ↔ 2 Thess 2:3 ↔ Rev 17:8,11 chain)
    • G2722 katechō (17x — distinguish "hold fast" vs. "restrain/hold back" senses)
    • G3485 naos (45x — map Pauline vs. non-Pauline vs. Revelation uses)
    • G458 anomia (15x), G459 anomos (10x) — for vocabulary cluster
    • G500 antichristos (5x, all Johannine)

  45. External corpus verification directives (from §5 above):

    • Verify naos=church Pauline pattern, AND steelman literal reading with 51 AD dating data
    • Verify whether 2 Thess 2 alone supports two-phase reading or imports it from Rev 13:3
    • Verify Matthew Henry chain Dan 11:36-37 → 1 Tim 4:1 lexical overlap
    • Verify Guinness's "commanded sin" anomia semantic register

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
greek-grammar-analysis (with heavy answer-question synthesis component — this is a who/what-is-it question that turns on Greek grammar + OT templates + NT parallel passages + five-view comparison)

Scoped: 2026-04-17 Folder: bible-studies/man-of-sin-2thess-2/

docs.title: Who Is the Man of Sin in 2 Thessalonians 2?
Introduction
docs.text: Paul warned the Thessalonians not to be shaken by claims that the day of the Lord had already come. Before that day, he said, two things must happen first: a "falling away" (apostasia) and the revealing of "the man of sin, the son of perdition" -- a figure who "opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God... so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God" (2 Thess 2:3-4). Who is this figure? Paul gives five titles for one entity, anchors him in Daniel, describes his operation in stages, and promises his destruction by the brightness of Christ's coming.

docs.title: Five Titles, One Figure
docs.text:
Paul uses five names across the pericope, all linked by the definite article and relative pronouns so that they refer to the same entity:

  • The man of lawlessness / man of sin (ho anthropos tes anomias / hamartias, v.3)
  • The son of perdition (ho huios tes apoleias, v.3)
  • The one opposing and exalting himself (v.4)
  • The lawless one (ho anomos, v.8) -- the only articular singular anomos in the NT
  • The one whose coming is... (v.9)

The textual variant "sin" vs. "lawlessness" at v.3 does not matter theologically: 1 John 3:4 equates the two ("sin is lawlessness"). The figure's defining characteristic is opposition to divine law, not merely moral failure.

docs.title: The Daniel 11:36 Lexical Bridge
docs.text:
Paul is quoting Daniel. LXX-Theodotion of Daniel 11:36 reads "he shall be exalted and magnified above every god" (epi panta theon); 2 Thess 2:4 reads "exalting himself above every so-called god or object of worship" (epi panta legomenon Theon). The phrase epi panta... theon is verbatim. Paul expands it with legomenon ("so-called") and sebasma ("object of worship"), broadening the scope from actual deities to anything venerated.

This means the man of sin is the NT actualization of Daniel 11:36's self-exalting king -- a king who "does according to his will," prospers until the indignation is accomplished, and is broken "without hand" (Dan 8:25). The destruction is by divine intervention, not by human warfare.

docs.title: The Judas Parallel Fixes the Character
docs.text:
The title huios tes apoleias ("son of perdition") appears only twice in the NT: of Judas in John 17:12, and of the man of sin in 2 Thess 2:3. The Semitic "son of X" idiom specifies destiny and character. In its Judas application, it names one who was:

  • Chosen as an apostle -- an insider.
  • Who turned against Christ -- a betrayer from within.
  • Whose fall fulfilled Scripture -- destined for destruction.

The man of sin inherits this entire profile. He is not a pagan external enemy; he is a betrayer from within God's covenant community. Revelation 17:8, 11 extends the apoleia destiny to the beast: "the beast... goeth into perdition." Judas, the man of sin, and the beast share the same destruction-destiny chain.

docs.title: The Temple of God (naos)
docs.text:
Paul uses naos tou Theou five times. Four refer to the church / believers:

  • 1 Corinthians 3:16-17 -- "ye are the temple of God"
  • 1 Corinthians 6:19 -- your body is a temple
  • 2 Corinthians 6:16 -- "ye are the temple of the living God"
  • Ephesians 2:21 -- the church is a "holy temple in the Lord"

The fifth is the disputed 2 Thess 2:4. Every uncontested Pauline use is ecclesial-figurative. The principle "author's own pattern carries heavy weight" supports the figurative reading: the man of sin operates within the church, not inside a rebuilt Jerusalem temple. The literal reading has contextual force (the Herodian temple still stood in AD 51), but requires Paul to mean something at 2 Thess 2:4 he means nowhere else.

The figurative reading also harmonizes with the Judas-parallel: the figure usurps authority from within God's dwelling, just as Judas betrayed from within the twelve.

docs.title: The Counter-Christ Pattern
docs.text:
Paul has systematically constructed the man of sin as the inversion of Christ:

Christ Man of Sin Has a parousia (2 Thess 2:1, 8) Has a parousia (2:9) -- same word Is revealed (apokalypto) Is revealed (apokalypto) -- same verb Demonstrated by God with signs (Acts 2:22) Demonstrates himself as God with lying signs (2:4, 2:9) -- same verb (apodeiknymi) Mystery of godliness (1 Tim 3:16) Mystery of lawlessness (2:7) God's energeia works in believers Satan's energeia works in him (2:9)

This is not incidental language. The man of sin claims everything Christ has -- revelation, parousia, accreditation by signs, energy-source, temple-seat -- but with satanic rather than divine backing. He is a counterfeit-Christ at every level.

docs.title: The Four-Stage Temporal Chain
docs.text:
Paul's grammar in vv.6-8 forces a temporally extended arc that no single-century reading can collapse:

  1. "Already working" (v.7, ede energeitai) -- the mystery was germinating in Paul's own day (c. AD 51).
  2. Restrainer holds back -- "only he who now letteth... until he be taken out of the way."
  3. "Then shall he be revealed" (v.8, future passive) -- the revelation follows the removal.
  4. "Whom the Lord shall consume... and destroy" (v.8) -- destruction at the parousia.

This chain is non-collapsible. An all-future reading must explain how the mystery was "already" working in AD 51. An all-past reading must explain how the figure is destroyed by a future parousia. The grammar insists on a many-century arc from germination to consummation.

1 John reinforces this: "many antichrists" are already in the world (1 Jn 2:18), paired with the antichrist who is coming -- distributed-plural contemporaries plus a singular climactic figure still future.

docs.title: The Destruction Is Eschatological
docs.text:
Verse 8 describes the destruction using two parousia-instruments:

  • "The breath of his mouth" -- directly from LXX Isaiah 11:4, the messianic destroyer-of-the-wicked passage.
  • "The brightness of his coming" -- epiphaneia + parousia, Paul's standard vocabulary for Christ's visible second advent (1 Tim 6:14; 2 Tim 4:1, 8; Titus 2:13).

No 1st-century candidate was destroyed by Christ's visible return. Nero suicided in AD 68 under Roman political pressure. Antiochus IV died of illness in 164 BC. Caligula was assassinated in AD 41. This is the decisive failure of the preterist and Caligula-type readings: the instrument of destruction Paul specifies never occurred in the 1st century.

docs.title: The Five Views Ranked
docs.text:
1. Historicist (Reformation tradition) -- strongest fit. An apostate religio-political system rising from within Christendom after a foretold falling away, claiming divine prerogatives, operating across many generations, destroyed at Christ's return. Fits all 8 textual markers. The TYPE is biblically secure; which specific historical institution fulfills the type is a secondary question beyond 2 Thess 2 alone.

  2. Dispensational Futurist -- second-strongest. A single future individual in a rebuilt Jerusalem temple. Handles the singularity markers well, strains at "already working" (a 20-century gap is substantial) and at "Holy Spirit as restrainer" (never so identified elsewhere).

  3. Idealist. Honors the "already" and the "many antichrists" register, but cannot bear the climactic singular revelation and specific parousia-destruction.

  4. Preterist. Explicit failure at v.8 -- no 1st-century candidate was destroyed by Christ's visible return.

  5. Roman Emperor (Caligula-type). Three explicit failures: a pagan cannot apostatize from a faith he never held; no emperor was destroyed by the parousia; cannot parallel Revelation 17's multi-century MYSTERY BABYLON.

docs.title: What Scripture Establishes with High Confidence
docs.text:
• The figure is church-internal, not pagan-external (apostasia + Judas parallel + Pauline naos pattern).
  • The figure is OT-templated on Daniel 11:36 via verbatim Theodotion linkage.
  • The figure is trans-temporal (already operative in AD 51 + climactic revelation + parousia destruction).
  • The figure is a counter-Christ structurally (parousia / revelation / accreditation / mystery / energy-source all inverted).
  • The figure is destroyed by Christ's visible coming, ruling out preterist and Caligula identifications.
  • The figure integrates political and religious functions (paralleling Revelation 13's two beasts), favoring an institutional-systemic rather than purely individual identity.

docs.title: What Scripture Does Not Decisively Determine
docs.text:
• The specific historical institution fulfilling the type.
  • The restrainer's identity (Roman state + Emperor is the best-attested candidate; Paul reserved the name for oral instruction in v.5).
  • Whether naos is the church (stronger Pauline inference) or the Jerusalem temple (contextual steelman).
  • Whether the figure is a single future person, a multi-generational succession, or a perennial principle with a climactic culmination.

docs.title: Pastoral Application
docs.text:
Paul wrote to calm an agitated church, not to alarm it. The pericope's purpose is steadiness under eschatological pressure.

  • Vigilance against apostasy. The threat is from within, not from pagan outsiders. Hold fast sound doctrine (2 Tim 1:13; Jude 3).
  • Love of the truth. Those who are preserved from the figure's deception "received the love of the truth" (2:10). Deception succeeds where affection for truth fails.
  • Discernment of signs and wonders. False miracles are real but false. Test every sign by doctrine (Deut 13:1-3; Matt 24:24).
  • Confidence in the parousia. The man of sin's operation is real but bounded by divine kairos. Christ will consume him by the breath of his mouth and destroy him by the brightness of his coming. Until then, saints stand fast and hold the traditions they have been taught (2 Thess 2:15).

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
The biblical data point most convincingly to an apostate religio-political system arising from within the Christian covenant community after a prophesied falling away, claiming divine prerogatives, operating across many generations under satanic empowerment, and destroyed only at Christ's visible return -- the historicist reading's structural profile.

The figure is one: five Pauline titles, one entity. He is from within: apostasia + Judas parallel + Pauline naos pattern. He is Daniel's self-exalting king actualized: verbatim LXX-Theodotion linkage. He is trans-temporal: already working + climactic revelation + parousia destruction. He is a counter-Christ: parousia, revelation, signs, mystery, energy-source all inverted. He is destroyed by the brightness of Christ's coming -- ruling out every 1st-century candidate.

Scripture identifies the TYPE with high confidence; specific historical identification requires historical-fulfillment evidence beyond 2 Thess 2 alone. The pastoral task remains regardless: vigilance, love of truth, refusal of lying wonders, and confidence in Christ's certain victory at his parousia.

Based on the full technical study completed 2026-04-17

docs.title: EXTERNAL CORPUS VERIFICATION — Man of Sin (2 Thess 2:3-12)
docs.text: Lead claims from 00-references.md, verified against biblical/tool data (no interpretive adjudication — that's the analysis agent's job).

docs.title: LEAD 1 — Bohr/Newton/Clarke/Froom: "Temple of God = Christian Church"
docs.text:
Verification route: (a) Pauline naos corpus pattern; (b) proximity of 2 Thess 51 AD to standing Herodian temple (literal counter-steelman); (c) Eph 2:19-22 + 1 Pet 2:4-5 building-metaphor chain cited by Bohr.

Data: - Pauline naos pattern is 4-of-5 figurative: 1 Cor 3:16-17, 1 Cor 6:19, 2 Cor 6:16, Eph 2:21 all apply naos to the church or individual believers. 2 Thess 2:4 is the fifth Pauline token (disputed). - Eph 2:19-22 — greek_parser.py output: "τὸν ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ" appears in 1 Cor 3:16-17; Eph 2:21 uses εἰς ναὸν ἅγιον ἐν Κυρίῳ — growing/organic building register with apostles/prophets as foundation, Christ as cornerstone (ἀκρογωνιαῖος). - 1 Pet 2:4-5 — "lively stones, spiritual house" (οἶκος πνευματικός) — parallel but uses οἶκος not ναός. - 2 Thess dating: ~51 AD (Paul's second missionary journey). Herodian temple stood until AD 70. Caligula statue-crisis was AD 40. - Matt 24:15 Olivet citation (by Jesus, ~30 AD) presupposes a future abomination in the holy place — the first Christian audience read 2 Thess 2:4 through Olivet.

Biblical weight of the claim: - PRO figurative: Pauline author-internal pattern strongly confirmed; Eph 2:21 + 1 Pet 2:5 parallel stream; Heb 3:6 "whose house are we"; Acts 7:47-50 "God dwelleth not in houses made with hands." - PRO literal: the 51-AD living-temple; the Caligula precedent; Matt 24:15 pendant; Rev 11:1-2 measured naos. - Status: both readings have biblical warrant; no single datum forces the choice. Analysis agent must weigh author-consistency-principle against historical-proximity.

docs.title: LEAD 2 — Bohr's "Two-phase papal rule with deadly-wound interval"
docs.text:
Claim: The man of sin rules for 1260 years, receives a deadly wound (Rev 13:3), interval, heals, returns to rule again.

Verification route: Does 2 Thess 2 itself support two-phase reading, or is this imported from Rev 13:3?

Data: - 2 Thess 2:6-8 gives a four-stage timeline: (1) mystery-already-works; (2) restrainer-removed; (3) revealed; (4) destroyed by parousia. - No textual datum in 2 Thess 2 inserts a "wound interval" between stages 3 and 4. - The "wound" concept is uniquely Rev 13:3 (μίαν ἐκ τῶν κεφαλῶν αὐτοῦ ὡς ἐσφαγμένην εἰς θάνατον, καὶ ἡ πληγὴ τοῦ θανάτου αὐτοῦ ἐθεραπεύθη).

Biblical weight: - 2 Thess 2 alone supports a single-arc progression (already-now → revealed → destroyed). Two-phase reading is a Rev-13 overlay, not a 2 Thess 2 text-derivation. - This does NOT make the two-phase reading wrong — Rev 13:3 is canonical — but it DOES mean the two-phase claim is NOT derivable from 2 Thess 2's own text. It is a Revelation-derived hermeneutical extension.

docs.title: LEAD 3 — Matthew Henry: Dan 11:36-37 → 1 Tim 4:1-3 → papal celibacy / Mahuzzim saints
docs.text:
Claim: "Nor shall he regard the desire of women" (Dan 11:37) = celibacy; "god of forces" (Mahuzzim, Dan 11:38) = saint-veneration; 1 Tim 4:3 "forbidding to marry" = the same apostasy the Antichrist embodies.

Verification route: (a) Hebrew of Dan 11:37 chemdat nashim; (b) Hebrew of Dan 11:38 ma'uzzim; (c) Greek of 1 Tim 4:3 κωλυόντων γαμεῖν vs. 2 Thess 2:3 ἀποστασία.

Data: - Hebrew chemdat nashim (Dan 11:37) — literally "desire of women." Semantic range: (i) natural affection for women / marriage bond; (ii) one specific desired woman (a lover/queen); (iii) by interpretive extension, abandoning normal sexual life. The Hebrew does NOT lexically entail celibacy-system; that reading is an interpretive possibility among several. - Hebrew ma'uzzim (Dan 11:38) — plural of ma'ōz "fortress, stronghold." Primary sense is military (god-of-fortresses). The saint/patron-veneration reading is by interpretive extension; the Hebrew does not itself denote saints. - 1 Tim 4:3 κωλυόντων γαμεῖν καὶ ἀπέχεσθαι βρωμάτων — forbidding to marry and commanding abstinence from foods. Explicit "forbidding marriage" register. - The Dan-11 chemdat nashim → 1 Tim 4:3 κωλύοντες γαμεῖν lexical bridge requires interpretive assumptions (Dan 11's phrase → broader celibacy meaning; 1 Tim 4's phrase applied to the Dan-11 figure). Neither bridge is linguistically necessary; both are interpretively available.

Biblical weight: - Dan 11:37-38 is semantically compatible with the celibacy/saint-veneration reading; it does NOT demand it. - 1 Tim 4:1-3 explicitly describes a latter-times apostasy with forbid-marriage/abstain-meat marks, providing a content-specification that CAN be applied to the Dan-11 figure. - The fusion of the two passages into a single identification rests on (a) reading 2 Thess 2:3 as the 1 Tim 4:1 apostasia; (b) reading Dan 11:36-37 as the 2 Thess 2:4 figure; (c) importing 1 Tim 4:3's content-marks into Dan 11's description. All three steps are biblically plausible; none is biblically forced.

docs.title: LEAD 4 — Guinness's "commanded sin" argument
docs.text:
Claim: anomia in 2 Thess 2 has institutional / commanded-sin register, not mere personal wickedness. This is what makes the man of sin identifiably the papal system rather than any generic wicked person.

Verification route: Does G458 anomia lexically/semantically connote organized/institutional opposition to law?

Data: - G458 anomia Louw-Nida: 88.18 "Act Lawlessly." - Range across NT: (a) concrete deeds ("iniquities" plural; Rom 4:7, Heb 8:12, Titus 2:14, Heb 10:17); (b) character/moral condition (Matt 23:28; 2 Cor 6:14 antithesis; Heb 1:9); (c) lawlessness personified in agents (Matt 7:23, 13:41; Rom 6:19); (d) eschatological intensification (Matt 24:12; 2 Thess 2:3, 7). - Dan 7:25 (Aramaic) — "think to change times and laws" (y-hashnāyāh zimnīn v-dāt). The little horn's programmatic law-opposition is institutional, not individual. This OT template supports a reading of anomia as organized/systemic. - 1 Jn 3:4 — ἡ ἁμαρτία ἐστὶν ἡ ἀνομία — equates sin and lawlessness; suggests they are not rigidly distinguished as individual-vs-institutional.

Biblical weight: - Guinness's reading is compatible with NT anomia range. The Dan 7:25 institutional-law-opposition template strongly supports it. - But the NT does not enforce an institutional-only reading — anomia ranges across individual/moral/eschatological scales. The Guinness argument is an available exegetical move, not a forced one.

docs.title: LEAD 5 — Newton/Clarke: "Pope in general, not any one pope" — succession vs. individual
docs.text:
Claim: Greek ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῆς ἀνομίας + definite article + singular does not force a single historical person; it allows a representative-of-class reading (pope-as-type, succession-as-one).

Verification route: (a) Greek anthrōpos + article + genitive construction grammatical range; (b) Dan 7:17 "four kings" = "four kingdoms" LXX precedent; (c) NT parallels where singular anthrōpos names a corporate or typological figure.

Data: - G444 anthrōpos — 557 NT tokens; base meaning "human being" generically. - With attributive genitive ("man of X"): denotes (i) individual characterized; (ii) class/type; or (iii) corporate/representative figure (per grammar-ref $GRAMMAR_REF/greek/anthropos-individual-vs-type.md). - Examples of corporate/typological anthrōpos in Paul: - Rom 5:12 "by one man (δι' ἑνὸς ἀνθρώπου) sin entered" — individual who is a corporate head. - 1 Cor 15:21 "by man came death, by man came resurrection" — two-Adam typology. - 1 Cor 15:45, 47 "first man Adam … last Adam / second man" — individual-as-type. - Rom 6:6 "old man / new man" — corporate aspectual identity. - Dan 7:17 LXX — τὰ τέσσαρα θηρία … τέσσαρες βασιλεῖς ("four kings"). v.23 translates this as "four kingdoms" (τέσσαρα βασιλεῖα). The text itself says "four kings" = "four kingdoms." This is the single explicit biblical precedent for king-singular-equals-kingdom-collective. - Dan 2 — "thou art the head of gold" (Nebuchadnezzar personally) followed by "after thee shall rise another kingdom" (collective kingdom). Same pattern: a personal king standing for a kingdom-collective.

Biblical weight: - The grammar alone (ὁ ἄνθρωπος + singular) does NOT force individual reading; anthrōpos-as-corporate-representative is a biblical pattern. - Daniel itself (7:17 → 7:23) establishes the precedent. - The Newton/Clarke succession reading is thus grammatically and biblically available; it is NOT foreclosed by anthrōpos singular. - Equally, the grammar does not force the succession reading — it allows both.

docs.title: LEAD 6 — EGW's "Mystery of Iniquity" as chapter title in EW/SH/SR/1SG
docs.text:
Claim: EGW's identification of the papal apostasy with the man of sin is load-bearing in historicist-Adventist tradition.

Status: This is historical-evidence commentary and interpretive tradition, NOT biblical exegesis.

Biblical verification policy: Treat as research lead only. Do NOT weight as independent evidence of biblical identification. The text is what it is; EGW's interpretation stands or falls by the exegetical route it takes.

What the biblical data establishes (independent of EGW): - 2 Thess 2:7 ἤδη ἐνεργεῖται — the mystery was operative in Paul's day. Any identification-claim must satisfy this. - 2 Thess 2:8 destruction at parousia — any identification-claim must satisfy the "destroyed at Christ's coming" condition. - The two bookends (already-active-1st-century + destroyed-at-parousia) together define the time-scope of the entity, which exceeds any single human life and fits either a succession or a continuing principle.

docs.title: LEAD 7 — LXX Dan 7:8 verbatim at Rev 13:5 — extend to 2 Thess 2:4 vs. Dan 11:36 / Isa 14 / Ezek 28
docs.text:
Claim: The verbatim LXX-tracer showing Rev 13:5 = Dan 7:8 LXX (στόμα λαλοῦν μεγάλα) should be run on 2 Thess 2:4.

Verification: - LXX Theod. Dan 11:36 — ὑψωθήσεται καὶ μεγαλυνθήσεται ἐπὶ πάντα θεόν ("he shall be exalted and magnified above every god"). - 2 Thess 2:4 — ὑπεραιρόμενος ἐπὶ πάντα λεγόμενον Θεὸν ἢ σέβασμα ("exalting himself above every so-called god or object of worship"). - Shared Greek: ἐπὶ πάντα θεόν (verbatim preposition + accusative + noun). Paul adds λεγόμενον ("so-called") as qualifier and ἢ σέβασμα as expansion. - Also: the LXX-Theod pair ὑψωθήσεται καὶ μεγαλυνθήσεται (fut. pass./mid.) is semantically matched by Paul's middle participle ὑπεραιρόμενος (middle-reflexive). - LXX Isa 14:13-14 — ἀναβήσομαι εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν … καθιῶ ἐν ὄρει ὑψηλῷ … ὅμοιος ἔσομαι τῷ ὑψίστῳ ("I will ascend into heaven … I will sit upon a lofty mountain … I will be like the Most High"). The καθιῶ (sit-motif) + divine-comparison register is conceptually shared with 2 Thess 2:4 εἰς τὸν ναὸν τοῦ Θεοῦ καθίσαι + ὅτι ἔστιν Θεός. - LXX Ezek 28:2 — θεὸς ἐγώ εἰμι, κατοικίαν θεοῦ κατῴκηκα ἐν καρδίᾳ θαλάσσης ("I am God, I inhabit a dwelling of God in the heart of the seas"). The θεὸς ἐγώ εἰμι claim is conceptually the direct antecedent to 2 Thess 2:4 ὅτι ἔστιν Θεός.

Verbal verdict: - 2 Thess 2:4 ἐπὶ πάντα … θεόν — verbatim LXX-Theod Dan 11:36. - 2 Thess 2:4 καθίσαι in divine seat — conceptually (not verbatim) Isa 14:13 καθιῶ and Ezek 28:2 κατοικίαν θεοῦ κατῴκηκα. - 2 Thess 2:4 ὅτι ἔστιν Θεός — conceptual (not verbatim) Ezek 28:2 θεὸς ἐγώ εἰμι.

Weight: Dan 11:36 is the primary verbatim OT template for 2 Thess 2:4; Isa 14 and Ezek 28 are thematic templates without verbatim overlap. This confirms Paul's direct dependence on the Dan-11 self-exalting-king portrait.

docs.title: LEAD 8 — Catholic counter-reading: Antichrist as future individual, not papal office
docs.text: Status: This reading is the SAME position as the dispensational-futurist steelman (view 3 in 00-references.md). Treat as one of the five views, not independent evidence.

docs.title: FIVE-VIEW BIBLICAL-FIT TABLE
docs.text:
Each view rated on the eight textual markers in 2 Thess 2:3-12. FIT = textually compatible; STRAIN = requires exegetical stretch; FAIL = text resists.

Marker Historicist (papal succession) Preterist (Nero / Jewish priesthood / Antiochus-type) Dispensational Futurist (future individual) Idealist (every-age principle) Roman-emperor (1st-century Caligula-type) 1. ἀποστασία precedes (v.3) FIT — post-apostolic Constantinian apostasy STRAIN — Jewish apostasy or priestly compromise FIT — future apostasy in last days FIT — perennial apostasy principle FAIL — pagan emperor can't apostatize from Christianity 2. ἄνθρωπος singular (v.3) FIT — anthrōpos-as-type (Dan 7:17 precedent) FIT — individual (Nero, high-priest, Antiochus) FIT — individual STRAIN — principle is harder to identify with singular FIT — individual emperor 3. self-exaltation "above every called god" (v.4) FIT — papal office's absolute religious claims STRAIN — Nero's divinity claims well-documented FIT — future Antichrist claims FIT — perennial self-deifying pattern FIT — Caligula's own self-deification 4. sitting in naos of God (v.4) FIT if naos = church; STRAIN if naos = Jerusalem FIT if naos = Jerusalem temple standing in 51 AD FIT — rebuilt future Jewish temple STRAIN — non-specific-location perennial principle FIT — Caligula's planned temple-statue 5. τὸ/ὁ κατέχον restrainer (v.6-7) FIT — pagan Rome state / emperor (removed 5th c.) FIT — Jewish political order or Roman state STRAIN — Holy Spirit (requires a rapture-removal exegetical move) STRAIN — any perennial restrainer difficult to specify FIT — Claudius personally (Suetonius-echo); removal allows another Caligula-type 6. mystery ἤδη ἐνεργεῖται (v.7) FIT — apostasy already incipient in apostolic era FIT — 1st century proto-gnosticism STRAIN — future figure requires a long already-arc FIT — principle is perennial STRAIN — specific-emperor seed is narrow 7. destroyed by parousia (v.8) FIT — final papal destruction at second coming FAIL — no single emperor/high-priest was destroyed by "brightness of parousia" FIT — single eschatological event STRAIN — perennial principle can't be destroyed at single parousia FAIL — emperor dies by natural/political means 8. functional-parallel to Rev 13 / Rev 17 (SP115) FIT (historicist reading of Rev 13 = papal system) STRAIN (preterist reads Rev 13 as Nero-era) FIT (future Antichrist = future beast) STRAIN (principle-beast thin) FAIL (emperor doesn't map cleanly to Rev 17 multi-century mystery)

docs.title: OBSERVATIONS ON THE EIGHT-MARKER TABLE
docs.text:
(1) Historicist view FITS all 8 markers without explicit FAIL but requires the figurative-naos reading. (2) Preterist has explicit FAIL at marker 7 (destruction by parousia) and STRAIN at markers 1 and 8. Neither Nero nor Jewish high-priesthood nor Antiochus was destroyed by "the brightness of the Lord's coming." (3) Dispensational Futurist FITS most markers but STRAINs markers 5 (Holy-Spirit-as-restrainer requires rapture-removal doctrine not in the text) and 6 (single-future-figure vs. "already working"). (4) Idealist FITS 1 and 3 and 6 but STRAINs on singularity markers (ἀποκαλυφθῆ singular subject; κριθῶσιν plural; single destruction). (5) Roman-emperor view has explicit FAIL at markers 1, 7, and 8. A pagan emperor cannot be the ho anthrōpos who FOLLOWS an ἀποστασία, cannot be destroyed by the brightness of the parousia, and cannot parallel Rev 17's multi-century MYSTERY-BABYLON.

The preterist and Roman-emperor views have explicit textual FAILs. The other three views have only STRAIN-type tension, each resolvable by additional exegetical moves.

Compiled: 2026-04-17. Research agent — no interpretive verdict; analysis agent's task.

docs.title: Nave's Entries
docs.text:
=========== TOPIC: ANTICHRIST ===========

docs.title: Text — 2 Thessalonians
docs.text: 2 Thessalonians 1:1 Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: 2 Thessalonians 1:2 Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. 2 Thessalonians 1:3 We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth; 2 Thessalonians 1:4 So that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure: 2 Thessalonians 1:5 [Which is] a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also suffer: 2 Thessalonians 1:6 Seeing [it is] a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you; 2 Thessalonians 1:7 And to you who are troubled rest with us, when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels, 2 Thessalonians 1:8 In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ: 2 Thessalonians 1:9 Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power; 2 Thessalonians 1:10 When he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired in all them that believe (because our testimony among you was believed) in that day. 2 Thessalonians 1:11 Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you worthy of [this] calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of [his] goodness, and the work of faith with power: 2 Thessalonians 1:12 That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ. 2 Thessalonians 2:1 Now we beseech you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and [by] our gathering together unto him, 2 Thessalonians 2:2 That ye be not soon shaken in mind, or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter as from us, as that the day of Christ is at hand. 2 Thessalonians 2:3 Let no man deceive you by any means: for [that day shall not come], except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition; 2 Thessalonians 2:4 Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God. 2 Thessalonians 2:5 Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you, I told you these things? 2 Thessalonians 2:6 And now ye know what withholdeth that he might be revealed in his time. 2 Thessalonians 2:7 For the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth [will let], until he be taken out of the way. 2 Thessalonians 2:8 And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming: 2 Thessalonians 2:9 [Even him], whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders, 2 Thessalonians 2:10 And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved. 2 Thessalonians 2:11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie: 2 Thessalonians 2:12 That they all might be damned who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness. 2 Thessalonians 2:13 But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth: 2 Thessalonians 2:14 Whereunto he called you by our gospel, to the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. 2 Thessalonians 2:15 Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle. 2 Thessalonians 2:16 Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given [us] everlasting consolation and good hope through grace, 2 Thessalonians 2:17 Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work. 2 Thessalonians 3:1 Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may have [free] course, and be glorified, even as [it is] with you: 2 Thessalonians 3:2 And that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all [men] have not faith. 2 Thessalonians 3:3 But the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep [you] from evil. 2 Thessalonians 3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord touching you, that ye both do and will do the things which we command you. 2 Thessalonians 3:5 And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ. 2 Thessalonians 3:6 Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which he received of us. 2 Thessalonians 3:7 For yourselves know how ye ought to follow us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you; 2 Thessalonians 3:8 Neither did we eat any man's bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you: 2 Thessalonians 3:9 Not because we have not power, but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us. 2 Thessalonians 3:10 For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat. 2 Thessalonians 3:11 For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies. 2 Thessalonians 3:12 Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread. 2 Thessalonians 3:13 But ye, brethren, be not weary in well doing. 2 Thessalonians 3:14 And if any man obey not our word by this epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed. 2 Thessalonians 3:15 Yet count [him] not as an enemy, but admonish [him] as a brother. 2 Thessalonians 3:16 Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord [be] with you all. 2 Thessalonians 3:17 The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write. 2 Thessalonians 3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen. 1 Thessalonians 4:1 Furthermore then we beseech you, brethren, and exhort [you] by the Lord Jesus, that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please God, [so] ye would abound more and more. 1 Thessalonians 4:2 For ye know what commandments we gave you by the Lord Jesus. 1 Thessalonians 4:3 For this is the will of God, [even] your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication: 1 Thessalonians 4:4 That every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour; 1 Thessalonians 4:5 Not in the lust of concupiscence, even as the Gentiles which know not God: 1 Thessalonians 4:6 That no [man] go beyond and defraud his brother in [any] matter: because that the Lord [is] the avenger of all such, as we also have forewarned you and testified. 1 Thessalonians 4:7 For God hath not called us unto uncleanness, but unto holiness. 1 Thessalonians 4:8 He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit. 1 Thessalonians 4:9 But as touching brotherly love ye need not that I write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another. 1 Thessalonians 4:10 And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more; 1 Thessalonians 4:11 And that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you; 1 Thessalonians 4:12 That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and [that] ye may have lack of nothing. 1 Thessalonians 4:13 But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope. 1 Thessalonians 4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him. 1 Thessalonians 4:15 For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive [and] remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep. 1 Thessalonians 4:16 For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 Then we which are alive [and] remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord. 1 Thessalonians 4:18 Wherefore comfort one another with these words. 1 Thessalonians 5:1 But of the times and the seasons, brethren, ye have no need that I write unto you. 1 Thessalonians 5:2 For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night. 1 Thessalonians 5:3 For when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child; and they shall not escape. 1 Thessalonians 5:4 But ye, brethren, are not in darkness, that that day should overtake you as a thief. 1 Thessalonians 5:5 Ye are all the children of light, and the children of the day: we are not of the night, nor of darkness. 1 Thessalonians 5:6 Therefore let us not sleep, as [do] others; but let us watch and be sober. 1 Thessalonians 5:7 For they that sleep sleep in the night; and they that be drunken are drunken in the night. 1 Thessalonians 5:8 But let us, who are of the day, be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love; and for an helmet, the hope of salvation. 1 Thessalonians 5:9 For God hath not appointed us to wrath, but to obtain salvation by our Lord Jesus Christ, 1 Thessalonians 5:10 Who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him. 1 Thessalonians 5:11 Wherefore comfort yourselves together, and edify one another, even as also ye do. 1 Thessalonians 5:12 And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you; 1 Thessalonians 5:13 And to esteem them very highly in love for their work's sake. [And] be at peace among yourselves. 1 Thessalonians 5:14 Now we exhort you, brethren, warn them that are unruly, comfort the feebleminded, support the weak, be patient toward all [men]. 1 Thessalonians 5:15 See that none render evil for evil unto any [man]; but ever follow that which is good, both among yourselves, and to all [men]. 1 Thessalonians 5:16 Rejoice evermore. 1 Thessalonians 5:17 Pray without ceasing. 1 Thessalonians 5:18 In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you. 1 Thessalonians 5:19 Quench not the Spirit. 1 Thessalonians 5:20 Despise not prophesyings. 1 Thessalonians 5:21 Prove all things; hold fast that which is good. 1 Thessalonians 5:22 Abstain from all appearance of evil. 1 Thessalonians 5:23 And the very God of peace sanctify you wholly; and [I pray God] your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. 1 Thessalonians 5:24 Faithful [is] he that calleth you, who also will do [it]. 1 Thessalonians 5:25 Brethren, pray for us. 1 Thessalonians 5:26 Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss. 1 Thessalonians 5:27 I charge you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren. 1 Thessalonians 5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you. Amen.

docs.title: Text — Daniel
docs.text: Daniel 7:1 In the first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon Daniel had a dream and visions of his head upon his bed: then he wrote the dream, [and] told the sum of the matters. Daniel 7:2 Daniel spake and said, I saw in my vision by night, and, behold, the four winds of the heaven strove upon the great sea. Daniel 7:3 And four great beasts came up from the sea, diverse one from another. Daniel 7:4 The first [was] like a lion, and had eagle's wings: I beheld till the wings thereof were plucked, and it was lifted up from the earth, and made stand upon the feet as a man, and a man's heart was given to it. Daniel 7:5 And behold another beast, a second, like to a bear, and it raised up itself on one side, and [it had] three ribs in the mouth of it between the teeth of it: and they said thus unto it, Arise, devour much flesh. Daniel 7:6 After this I beheld, and lo another, like a leopard, which had upon the back of it four wings of a fowl; the beast had also four heads; and dominion was given to it. Daniel 7:7 After this I saw in the night visions, and behold a fourth beast, dreadful and terrible, and strong exceedingly; and it had great iron teeth: it devoured and brake in pieces, and stamped the residue with the feet of it: and it [was] diverse from all the beasts that [were] before it; and it had ten horns. Daniel 7:8 I considered the horns, and, behold, there came up among them another little horn, before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots: and, behold, in this horn [were] eyes like the eyes of man, and a mouth speaking great things. Daniel 7:9 I beheld till the thrones were cast down, and the Ancient of days did sit, whose garment [was] white as snow, and the hair of his head like the pure wool: his throne [was like] the fiery flame, [and] his wheels [as] burning fire. Daniel 7:10 A fiery stream issued and came forth from before him: thousand thousands ministered unto him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him: the judgment was set, and the books were opened. Daniel 7:11 I beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spake: I beheld [even] till the beast was slain, and his body destroyed, and given to the burning flame. Daniel 7:12 As concerning the rest of the beasts, they had their dominion taken away: yet their lives were prolonged for a season and time. Daniel 7:13 I saw in the night visions, and, behold, [one] like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven, and came to the Ancient of days, and they brought him near before him. Daniel 7:14 And there was given him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom, that all people, nations, and languages, should serve him: his dominion [is] an everlasting dominion, which shall not pass away, and his kingdom [that] which shall not be destroyed. Daniel 7:15 I Daniel was grieved in my spirit in the midst of [my] body, and the visions of my head troubled me. Daniel 7:16 I came near unto one of them that stood by, and asked him the truth of all this. So he told me, and made me know the interpretation of the things. Daniel 7:17 These great beasts, which are four, [are] four kings, [which] shall arise out of the earth. Daniel 7:18 But the saints of the most High shall take the kingdom, and possess the kingdom for ever, even for ever and ever. Daniel 7:19 Then I would know the truth of the fourth beast, which was diverse from all the others, exceeding dreadful, whose teeth [were of] iron, and his nails [of] brass; [which] devoured, brake in pieces, and stamped the residue with his feet; Daniel 7:20 And of the ten horns that [were] in his head, and [of] the other which came up, and before whom three fell; even [of] that horn that had eyes, and a mouth that spake very great things, whose look [was] more stout than his fellows. Daniel 7:21 I beheld, and the same horn made war with the saints, and prevailed against them; Daniel 7:22 Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment was given to the saints of the most High; and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom. Daniel 7:23 Thus he said, The fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth, which shall be diverse from all kingdoms, and shall devour the whole earth, and shall tread it down, and break it in pieces. Daniel 7:24 And the ten horns out of this kingdom [are] ten kings [that] shall arise: and another shall rise after them; and he shall be diverse from the first, and he shall subdue three kings. Daniel 7:25 And he shall speak [great] words against the most High, and shall wear out the saints of the most High, and think to change times and laws: and they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and the dividing of time. Daniel 7:26 But the judgment shall sit, and they shall take away his dominion, to consume and to destroy [it] unto the end. Daniel 7:27 And the kingdom and dominion, and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heaven, shall be given to the people of the saints of the most High, whose kingdom [is] an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey him. Daniel 7:28 Hitherto [is] the end of the matter. As for me Daniel, my cogitations much troubled me, and my countenance changed in me: but I kept the matter in my heart. Daniel 8:1 In the third year of the reign of king Belshazzar a vision appeared unto me, [even unto] me Daniel, after that which appeared unto me at the first. Daniel 8:2 And I saw in a vision; and it came to pass, when I saw, that I [was] at Shushan [in] the palace, which [is] in the province of Elam; and I saw in a vision, and I was by the river of Ulai. Daniel 8:3 Then I lifted up mine eyes, and saw, and, behold, there stood before the river a ram which had [two] horns: and the [two] horns [were] high; but one [was] higher than the other, and the higher came up last. Daniel 8:4 I saw the ram pushing westward, and northward, and southward; so that no beasts might stand before him, neither [was there any] that could deliver out of his hand; but he did according to his will, and became great. Daniel 8:5 And as I was considering, behold, an he goat came from the west on the face of the whole earth, and touched not the ground: and the goat [had] a notable horn between his eyes. Daniel 8:6 And he came to the ram that had [two] horns, which I had seen standing before the river, and ran unto him in the fury of his power. Daniel 8:7 And I saw him come close unto the ram, and he was moved with choler against him, and smote the ram, and brake his two horns: and there was no power in the ram to stand before him, but he cast him down to the ground, and stamped upon him: and there was none that could deliver the ram out of his hand. Daniel 8:8 Therefore the he goat waxed very great: and when he was strong, the great horn was broken; and for it came up four notable ones toward the four winds of heaven. Daniel 8:9 And out of one of them came forth a little horn, which waxed exceeding great, toward the south, and toward the east, and toward the pleasant [land]. Daniel 8:10 And it waxed great, [even] to the host of heaven; and it cast down [some] of the host and of the stars to the ground, and stamped upon them. Daniel 8:11 Yea, he magnified [himself] even to the prince of the host, and by him the daily [sacrifice] was taken away, and the place of his sanctuary was cast down. Daniel 8:12 And an host was given [him] against the daily [sacrifice] by reason of transgression, and it cast down the truth to the ground; and it practised, and prospered. Daniel 8:13 Then I heard one saint speaking, and another saint said unto that certain [saint] which spake, How long [shall be] the vision [concerning] the daily [sacrifice], and the transgression of desolation, to give both the sanctuary and the host to be trodden under foot? Daniel 8:14 And he said unto me, Unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed. Daniel 8:15 And it came to pass, when I, [even] I Daniel, had seen the vision, and sought for the meaning, then, behold, there stood before me as the appearance of a man. Daniel 8:16 And I heard a man's voice between [the banks of] Ulai, which called, and said, Gabriel, make this [man] to understand the vision. Daniel 8:17 So he came near where I stood: and when he came, I was afraid, and fell upon my face: but he said unto me, Understand, O son of man: for at the time of the end [shall be] the vision. Daniel 8:18 Now as he was speaking with me, I was in a deep sleep on my face toward the ground: but he touched me, and set me upright. Daniel 8:19 And he said, Behold, I will make thee know what shall be in the last end of the indignation: for at the time appointed the end [shall be]. Daniel 8:20 The ram which thou sawest having [two] horns [are] the kings of Media and Persia. Daniel 8:21 And the rough goat [is] the king of Grecia: and the great horn that [is] between his eyes [is] the first king. Daniel 8:22 Now that being broken, whereas four stood up for it, four kingdoms shall stand up out of the nation, but not in his power. Daniel 8:23 And in the latter time of their kingdom, when the transgressors are come to the full, a king of fierce countenance, and understanding dark sentences, shall stand up. Daniel 8:24 And his power shall be mighty, but not by his own power: and he shall destroy wonderfully, and shall prosper, and practise, and shall destroy the mighty and the holy people. Daniel 8:25 And through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand; and he shall magnify [himself] in his heart, and by peace shall destroy many: he shall also stand up against the Prince of princes; but he shall be broken without hand. Daniel 8:26 And the vision of the evening and the morning which was told [is] true: wherefore shut thou up the vision; for it [shall be] for many days. Daniel 8:27 And I Daniel fainted, and was sick [certain] days; afterward I rose up, and did the king's business; and I was astonished at the vision, but none understood [it]. Daniel 11:1 Also I in the first year of Darius the Mede, [even] I, stood to confirm and to strengthen him. Daniel 11:2 And now will I shew thee the truth. Behold, there shall stand up yet three kings in Persia; and the fourth shall be far richer than [they] all: and by his strength through his riches he shall stir up all against the realm of Grecia. Daniel 11:3 And a mighty king shall stand up, that shall rule with great dominion, and do according to his will. Daniel 11:4 And when he shall stand up, his kingdom shall be broken, and shall be divided toward the four winds of heaven; and not to his posterity, nor according to his dominion which he ruled: for his kingdom shall be plucked up, even for others beside those. Daniel 11:5 And the king of the south shall be strong, and [one] of his princes; and he shall be strong above him, and have dominion; his dominion [shall be] a great dominion. Daniel 11:6 And in the end of years they shall join themselves together; for the king's daughter of the south shall come to the king of the north to make an agreement: but she shall not retain the power of the arm; neither shall he stand, nor his arm: but she shall be given up, and they that brought her, and he that begat her, and he that strengthened her in [these] times. Daniel 11:7 But out of a branch of her roots shall [one] stand up in his estate, which shall come with an army, and shall enter into the fortress of the king of the north, and shall deal against them, and shall prevail: Daniel 11:8 And shall also carry captives into Egypt their gods, with their princes, [and] with their precious vessels of silver and of gold; and he shall continue [more] years than the king of the north. Daniel 11:9 So the king of the south shall come into [his] kingdom, and shall return into his own land. Daniel 11:10 But his sons shall be stirred up, and shall assemble a multitude of great forces: and [one] shall certainly come, and overflow, and pass through: then shall he return, and be stirred up, [even] to his fortress. Daniel 11:11 And the king of the south shall be moved with choler, and shall come forth and fight with him, [even] with the king of the north: and he shall set forth a great multitude; but the multitude shall be given into his hand. Daniel 11:12 [And] when he hath taken away the multitude, his heart shall be lifted up; and he shall cast down [many] ten thousands: but he shall not be strengthened [by it]. Daniel 11:13 For the king of the north shall return, and shall set forth a multitude greater than the former, and shall certainly come after certain years with a great army and with much riches. Daniel 11:14 And in those times there shall many stand up against the king of the south: also the robbers of thy people shall exalt themselves to establish the vision; but they shall fall. Daniel 11:15 So the king of the north shall come, and cast up a mount, and take the most fenced cities: and the arms of the south shall not withstand, neither his chosen people, neither [shall there be any] strength to withstand. Daniel 11:16 But he that cometh against him shall do according to his own will, and none shall stand before him: and he shall stand in the glorious land, which by his hand shall be consumed. Daniel 11:17 He shall also set his face to enter with the strength of his whole kingdom, and upright ones with him; thus shall he do: and he shall give him the daughter of women, corrupting her: but she shall not stand [on his side], neither be for him. Daniel 11:18 After this shall he turn his face unto the isles, and shall take many: but a prince for his own behalf shall cause the reproach offered by him to cease; without his own reproach he shall cause [it] to turn upon him. Daniel 11:19 Then he shall turn his face toward the fort of his own land: but he shall stumble and fall, and not be found. Daniel 11:20 Then shall stand up in his estate a raiser of taxes [in] the glory of the kingdom: but within few days he shall be destroyed, neither in anger, nor in battle. Daniel 11:21 And in his estate shall stand up a vile person, to whom they shall not give the honour of the kingdom: but he shall come in peaceably, and obtain the kingdom by flatteries. Daniel 11:22 And with the arms of a flood shall they be overflown from before him, and shall be broken; yea, also the prince of the covenant. Daniel 11:23 And after the league [made] with him he shall work deceitfully: for he shall come up, and shall become strong with a small people. Daniel 11:24 He shall enter peaceably even upon the fattest places of the province; and he shall do [that] which his fathers have not done, nor his fathers' fathers; he shall scatter among them the prey, and spoil, and riches: [yea], and he shall forecast his devices against the strong holds, even for a time. Daniel 11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army; and the king of the south shall be stirred up to battle with a very great and mighty army; but he shall not stand: for they shall forecast devices against him. Daniel 11:26 Yea, they that feed of the portion of his meat shall destroy him, and his army shall overflow: and many shall fall down slain. Daniel 11:27 And both these kings' hearts [shall be] to do mischief, and they shall speak lies at one table; but it shall not prosper: for yet the end [shall be] at the time appointed. Daniel 11:28 Then shall he return into his land with great riches; and his heart [shall be] against the holy covenant; and he shall do [exploits], and return to his own land. Daniel 11:29 At the time appointed he shall return, and come toward the south; but it shall not be as the former, or as the latter. Daniel 11:30 For the ships of Chittim shall come against him: therefore he shall be grieved, and return, and have indignation against the holy covenant: so shall he do; he shall even return, and have intelligence with them that forsake the holy covenant. Daniel 11:31 And arms shall stand on his part, and they shall pollute the sanctuary of strength, and shall take away the daily [sacrifice], and they shall place the abomination that maketh desolate. Daniel 11:32 And such as do wickedly against the covenant shall he corrupt by flatteries: but the people that do know their God shall be strong, and do [exploits]. Daniel 11:33 And they that understand among the people shall instruct many: yet they shall fall by the sword, and by flame, by captivity, and by spoil, [many] days. Daniel 11:34 Now when they shall fall, they shall be holpen with a little help: but many shall cleave to them with flatteries. Daniel 11:35 And [some] of them of understanding shall fall, to try them, and to purge, and to make [them] white, [even] to the time of the end: because [it is] yet for a time appointed. Daniel 11:36 And the king shall do according to his will; and he shall exalt himself, and magnify himself above every god, and shall speak marvellous things against the God of gods, and shall prosper till the indignation be accomplished: for that that is determined shall be done. Daniel 11:37 Neither shall he regard the God of his fathers, nor the desire of women, nor regard any god: for he shall magnify himself above all. Daniel 11:38 But in his estate shall he honour the God of forces: and a god whom his fathers knew not shall he honour with gold, and silver, and with precious stones, and pleasant things. Daniel 11:39 Thus shall he do in the most strong holds with a strange god, whom he shall acknowledge [and] increase with glory: and he shall cause them to rule over many, and shall divide the land for gain. Daniel 11:40 And at the time of the end shall the king of the south push at him: and the king of the north shall come against him like a whirlwind, with chariots, and with horsemen, and with many ships; and he shall enter into the countries, and shall overflow and pass over. Daniel 11:41 He shall enter also into the glorious land, and many [countries] shall be overthrown: but these shall escape out of his hand, [even] Edom, and Moab, and the chief of the children of Ammon. Daniel 11:42 He shall stretch forth his hand also upon the countries: and the land of Egypt shall not escape. Daniel 11:43 But he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver, and over all the precious things of Egypt: and the Libyans and the Ethiopians [shall be] at his steps. Daniel 11:44 But tidings out of the east and out of the north shall trouble him: therefore he shall go forth with great fury to destroy, and utterly to make away many. Daniel 11:45 And he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end, and none shall help him. Daniel 12:1 And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation [even] to that same time: and at that time thy people shall be delivered, every one that shall be found written in the book. Daniel 12:2 And many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame [and] everlasting contempt. Daniel 12:3 And they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament; and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars for ever and ever. Daniel 12:4 But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words, and seal the book, [even] to the time of the end: many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased. Daniel 12:5 Then I Daniel looked, and, behold, there stood other two, the one on this side of the bank of the river, and the other on that side of the bank of the river. Daniel 12:6 And [one] said to the man clothed in linen, which [was] upon the waters of the river, How long [shall it be to] the end of these wonders? Daniel 12:7 And I heard the man clothed in linen, which [was] upon the waters of the river, when he held up his right hand and his left hand unto heaven, and sware by him that liveth for ever that [it shall be] for a time, times, and an half; and when he shall have accomplished to scatter the power of the holy people, all these [things] shall be finished. Daniel 12:8 And I heard, but I understood not: then said I, O my Lord, what [shall be] the end of these [things]? Daniel 12:9 And he said, Go thy way, Daniel: for the words [are] closed up and sealed till the time of the end. Daniel 12:10 Many shall be purified, and made white, and tried; but the wicked shall do wickedly: and none of the wicked shall understand; but the wise shall understand. Daniel 12:11 And from the time [that] the daily [sacrifice] shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, [there shall be] a thousand two hundred and ninety days. Daniel 12:12 Blessed [is] he that waiteth, and cometh to the thousand three hundred and five and thirty days. Daniel 12:13 But go thou thy way till the end [be]: for thou shalt rest, and stand in thy lot at the end of the days.

docs.title: Text — Isaiah/Ezekiel
docs.text: Isaiah 11:1 And there shall come forth a rod out of the stem of Jesse, and a Branch shall grow out of his roots: Isaiah 11:2 And the spirit of the LORD shall rest upon him, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and of the fear of the LORD; Isaiah 11:3 And shall make him of quick understanding in the fear of the LORD: and he shall not judge after the sight of his eyes, neither reprove after the hearing of his ears: Isaiah 11:4 But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked. Isaiah 11:5 And righteousness shall be the girdle of his loins, and faithfulness the girdle of his reins. Isaiah 11:6 The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together; and a little child shall lead them. Isaiah 11:7 And the cow and the bear shall feed; their young ones shall lie down together: and the lion shall eat straw like the ox. Isaiah 11:8 And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp, and the weaned child shall put his hand on the cockatrice' den. Isaiah 11:9 They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain: for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the LORD, as the waters cover the sea. Isaiah 11:10 And in that day there shall be a root of Jesse, which shall stand for an ensign of the people; to it shall the Gentiles seek: and his rest shall be glorious. Isaiah 11:11 And it shall come to pass in that day, [that] the Lord shall set his hand again the second time to recover the remnant of his people, which shall be left, from Assyria, and from Egypt, and from Pathros, and from Cush, and from Elam, and from Shinar, and from Hamath, and from the islands of the sea. Isaiah 11:12 And he shall set up an ensign for the nations, and shall assemble the outcasts of Israel, and gather together the dispersed of Judah from the four corners of the earth. Isaiah 11:13 The envy also of Ephraim shall depart, and the adversaries of Judah shall be cut off: Ephraim shall not envy Judah, and Judah shall not vex Ephraim. Isaiah 11:14 But they shall fly upon the shoulders of the Philistines toward the west; they shall spoil them of the east together: they shall lay their hand upon Edom and Moab; and the children of Ammon shall obey them. Isaiah 11:15 And the LORD shall utterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea; and with his mighty wind shall he shake his hand over the river, and shall smite it in the seven streams, and make [men] go over dryshod. Isaiah 11:16 And there shall be an highway for the remnant of his people, which shall be left, from Assyria; like as it was to Israel in the day that he came up out of the land of Egypt. Isaiah 14:1 For the LORD will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose Israel, and set them in their own land: and the strangers shall be joined with them, and they shall cleave to the house of Jacob. Isaiah 14:2 And the people shall take them, and bring them to their place: and the house of Israel shall possess them in the land of the LORD for servants and handmaids: and they shall take them captives, whose captives they were; and they shall rule over their oppressors. Isaiah 14:3 And it shall come to pass in the day that the LORD shall give thee rest from thy sorrow, and from thy fear, and from the hard bondage wherein thou wast made to serve, Isaiah 14:4 That thou shalt take up this proverb against the king of Babylon, and say, How hath the oppressor ceased! the golden city ceased! Isaiah 14:5 The LORD hath broken the staff of the wicked, [and] the sceptre of the rulers. Isaiah 14:6 He who smote the people in wrath with a continual stroke, he that ruled the nations in anger, is persecuted, [and] none hindereth. Isaiah 14:7 The whole earth is at rest, [and] is quiet: they break forth into singing.
